Statement.Description: The SpaceMouse statement is only used together with the SpaceMouse and the SpaceAlign software package. This statement opens the SpaceMouse Control panel and activate the Space Mouse with the selected Mouse Set and Mode. Refer to the SpaceMouse section for further information. Note: Before using this statement in a sequence pleas make sure, that an Exit button is defined. It is advised to the EXIT function to Space Mouse Button 8. If no Exit button is defined, the sequence cannot be finished.
Statement.Name: SpaceMouse

Parameter.Name: Mouse Set
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select a space mouse parameter set from drop down list. The space mouse parameter set has to be defined before within the SpaceMouse configuration panel.

Parameter.Name: Mode
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select display mode from drop down list; display modes are described above: no display: no dialog is shown; info only: an info dialog is shown with the button functions; positions: an info dialog with button functions and current SpaceMouse position is shown.

Parameter.Name: Message
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter text shown in dialog.

Parameter.Name: Width( <0=prev)
Parameter.Description: Width of panel (default 0 = use recent set size) Minimum value of height is 256.

Parameter.Name: Height (<0=prev)
Parameter.Description: Height of panel (default 0 = use recent set size) Minimum value of height is 256.

Statement.Description: This statement closes the selected IMAQ picture.
Statement.Name: CloseIMAQPicture

Parameter.Name: Window Number (0...15)
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter number of picture to close.

Statement.Description: This statement detects edges in a given grayscale image and returns the number of edges found and the edges coordinates. The parameters can be set in the Advanced Edge Detection Panel.
Statement.Name: DetectEdgesAdvanced

Parameter.Name: Show picture
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Choose if picture will be shown: Show=activate show picture panel; Hide=Don?t show the picture.

Parameter.Name: Edges Coordinates
Parameter.Description: returns the coordinates of the detected edges.

Parameter.Name: Number of Edges
Parameter.Description: specifies the number of edges found.

Statement.Description: This statement extracts the single color planes red, green and blue from a color image and returns references to the three resulting grayscale images. Note: This statement only support U32 RGB images.
Statement.Name: ExtractSingleColorPlane

Parameter.Name: Image In
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select camera from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Red Image Out
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Returns flattened reference to an image.

Parameter.Name: Green Image Out
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Returns flattened reference to an image.

Parameter.Name: Blue Image Out
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Returns flattened reference to an image.

Parameter.Name: Grayscale Image Out
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Returns flattened reference to an image.

Statement.Description: This statement grabs a picture and returns a reference to the grabbed image.
Statement.Name: Grab

Parameter.Name: Camera
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select camera from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Image Out
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Returns flattened reference to an image.

Statement.Description: This statement copies the image from the ImageSrc reference to the ImageDst reference.
Statement.Name: IMAQCopy

Parameter.Name: ImageSrc
Parameter.Description: This is the image reference (source) which should be copied to another reference (ImageDst).

Parameter.Name: ImageDst
Parameter.Description: This is the image reference where the ImageIn picture should be copied to. If the ImageDst reference is left blank (""), a unique name is generated and the resulting reference is return in "ImageDst_Duplicate"

Parameter.Name: ImageDst_Duplicate
Parameter.Description: This returns the image reference of the image where the picture was copied. If the ImageDst was defined, this reference is a duplicate of the ImageDst refernce.

Statement.Description: This statement creates an image reference usable to reference an image in further processing steps.
Statement.Name: IMAQCreate

Parameter.Name: Image Name
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter image name.

Parameter.Name: Border Size
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Determines the width in pixels of the boarder to be created around the image.

Parameter.Name: Image Type
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select type of image from drop down list. (8 bits, 16 bits, Float, Complex, RGB, HSL)

Parameter.Name: Image
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns flattened reference to an image.

Statement.Description: This statement disposes one given or all image references.
Statement.Name: IMAQDispose

Parameter.Name: Image
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Flattened reference to an image.

Parameter.Name: All Images?
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select if all images should be closed: True= all images are closed; False=Closes image specified.

Statement.Description: Reads an image into an image reference previously created. If path is a name or a relative path it is interpreted as realtive to #SEA_Master_DATA# path, otherwise as an absolut path.
Statement.Name: IMAQReadFile

Parameter.Name: Image In
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Flattened reference to an image.

Parameter.Name: File Path
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Path and file name to an image.

Parameter.Name: Image Out
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns flattened reference to an image.

Statement.Description: This statement saves an image into a given file format.
Statement.Name: IMAQWriteFile

Parameter.Name: Image In
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Flattened reference to an image.

Parameter.Name: FileName
Parameter.Description: Parameter: File name to an image path is the data folder.

Parameter.Name: FileType
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select file format.

Statement.Description: This statement returns the x- and y-coordinate of the edge at the given index within the edges coordinates.
Statement.Name: IndexEdgesCoordinates

Parameter.Name: Edges Coordinates
Parameter.Description: returns the coordinates of the detected edges.

Parameter.Name: Index
Parameter.Description: specifies the number of edges found.

Statement.Description: Loads a correction file for a specific device and color plane. The file must contain a 2D, TAB delimited string array with exactly the same dimension size as the image size of the selected camera device. Correction files must be loaded for Red, Green and Blue color plane before correction can be activated by the "UseRGBCorrection" statement. Correction will be done by multiplying the corresponding pixel values of the grabbed R,G and B color plane and the appropriate correction image loaded with this statement.
Statement.Name: LoadCorrectionFile

Parameter.Name: Camera
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select camera from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: CorrectionPlane
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select the color plane to load the correction for

Parameter.Name: CorrectionFileName
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Filename to load from selected folder

Parameter.Name: BasePath
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select a base path from the drop down list. If "Path" is selected, parameter "Filename In" will be interpreted as an absolute path.

Statement.Description: This statement runs a pattern matching process with the given pattern on the given image and returns the x- and y-position as well as a matching score of the matched pattern. The position is corresponding to the middle of the pattern.
Statement.Name: MatchPatternAdvanced

Parameter.Name: Image In
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select camera from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Minimum Match Score
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter a value between 1 and 1000 for the minimum match score.

Parameter.Name: Show picture
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Choose if picture will be shown: Show=activate show picture panel; Hide=Don?t show the picture.

Statement.Description: Saves an image to a TAB delimited, 2D ASCII file. Before saving to file a per pixel calculation can be performed using the parameters "Pixel Operation [+, -, *, /]" and "Calculate Value".
Statement.Name: SaveImageToSpreadsheetFile

Parameter.Name: Image In
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Flattened reference to an image.

Parameter.Name: Filename In
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter the filename to save the ASCII file to.

Parameter.Name: Base Path
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select a base path from the drop down list. If "Path" is selected, parameter "Filename In" will be interpreted as an absolute path.

Parameter.Name: Pixel Operation
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Optional a calculating operation can be performed per pixel before saving

Parameter.Name: Calculate Value
Parameter.Description: Parameter: The value to calculate with if a pixel operation is selected.

Parameter.Name: Pixel Reciprocal
Parameter.Description: Parameter: If "True" the reciprocal value of each pixel is calculated and saved to file. If in addition a pixel operation is selected, this additional operation is calculated after building the reciprocal.

Statement.Description: Displays an image.
Statement.Name: ShowIMAQPicture

Parameter.Name: Image In
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Flattened reference to an image.

Parameter.Name: Title
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter title of picture.

Parameter.Name: Window Number (0...15)
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select a picture number between 0 and 15.

Statement.Description: This statement displays an image as picture for the given time interval.
Statement.Name: ShowPicture

Parameter.Name: Image In
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Flattened reference to an image.

Parameter.Name: Color Table
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter number of picture to close.Select a color table from the drop down list: Calculate, Grayscale or 256 Colors.

Parameter.Name: TimeOut(ms)
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter a timeout value for stopping the process.

Statement.Description: Enable or disable the use of color plane correction for a specific device. If enabled, the Red, Green and Blue plane will be corrected. Note: Before enabling correction values must be loaded for each color plane.
Statement.Name: UseRGBCorrection

Parameter.Name: Camera
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select camera from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Enable
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Set to Enable to use RGB correction. Set to Disable to not to use RGB correction

Statement.Description: Clear all currently loaded arrays from memory.
Statement.Name: ArrayHandling_ClearAll

Statement.Description: Copy one array into another. Both arrays must have identical sizes and dimensions. Use statements ArrayHandling_GetArraySizes and ArrayHandling_AddNewXYZArray to initialize a destination array before copying.
Statement.Name: ArrayHandling_CopyArray

Parameter.Name: SourceArrayName
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Name of the array to copy from

Parameter.Name: DestinationArrayName
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Name of the array to copy to

Parameter.Name: Copy OK?
Parameter.Description: Result: "True" if array found. "False" if array not found.

Statement.Description: Delete a single array from memory. Note: This statement does not delete any saved arrays
Statement.Name: ArrayHandling_DeleteArray

Parameter.Name: ArrayName
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Name of the array to be deleted from memory.

Parameter.Name: Found?
Parameter.Description: Result: "True" if array found. "False" if array not found.

Statement.Description: This statement gets the dimension sizes of an array that is currently loaded into memory
Statement.Name: ArrayHandling_GetArraySizes

Parameter.Name: ArrayName
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Name of the array

Parameter.Name: Row Size
Parameter.Description: Result: Size of array in dimension 1

Parameter.Name: Column size
Parameter.Description: Result: Size of array in dimension 2

Statement.Description: Load a previously saved array from file. Arrays are saved in binary format for performance reasons.
Statement.Name: ArrayHandling_LoadArray

Parameter.Name: BasePath
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select a base path from the drop down list. If "Path" is selected, parameter "Filename In" will be interpreted as an absolute path.

Parameter.Name: FileName
Parameter.Description: Parameter: List of saved arrays in selected folder. All files with extension "*.array" will be selectable.

Parameter.Name: TargetArrayName
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Name of array the saved array shall be loaded to. If an array with the given name is already loaded into memory it will be overwritten with the new array

Statement.Description: Save a currently loaded array to file. Arrays are saved in binary format for performance reasons.
Statement.Name: ArrayHandling_SaveArray

Parameter.Name: ArrayName
Parameter.Description: Parameter: List of all arrays currently loaded into memory

Parameter.Name: FileName
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Filename to save the array to. File extension will be set automatically to *.array

Parameter.Name: BasePath
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select a base path from the drop down list. If "Path" is selected, parameter "Filename In" will be interpreted as an absolute path.

Statement.Description: Add a new 1D numeric array to the array handling function. If an array with the given name already exists this statement will return "false" in the "Created" output
Statement.Name: ArrayHandling_AddNewArray1DDouble

Parameter.Name: ArrayName
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Name of the array to create

Parameter.Name: defaultValue
Parameter.Description: Parameter: All elements will be initialized with default value

Parameter.Name: dimension size
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Size of array in 1D

Parameter.Name: Created
Parameter.Description: Result: "True" if array was created. "False" if array was not created because it was already initialized with the same name.

Statement.Description: Add a new 2D numeric array to the array handling function. If an array with the given name already exists this statement will return "false" in the "Created" output
Statement.Name: ArrayHandling_AddNewArray2DDouble

Parameter.Name: ArrayName
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Name of the array to create

Parameter.Name: defaultValue
Parameter.Description: Parameter: All elements will be initialized with default value

Parameter.Name: row size
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Size of array in 1D

Parameter.Name: Created
Parameter.Description: Result: "True" if array was created. "False" if array was not created because it was already initialized with the same name.

Statement.Description: This statement converts an 1D array of numeric values to a string separated by delimiters. The formatting of the string as well as the delimiter can be set.
Statement.Name: ArrayHandling_ArrayToSeparatedString1DDouble

Parameter.Name: ArrayName
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Name of the array to convert

Parameter.Name: Format string
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Format string to be used for converting single values to strings. E.g. use %d for decimal values, %f for floating point values. Refer to the LabVIEW help for more informations about how to use format strings

Parameter.Name: delimiter (Tab)
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select the delimiter to be used. Delimiters can be SPACE, TAB, COMMA or SEMICOLON

Parameter.Name: CommaSeparatedString
Parameter.Description: Result: String of separated values

Statement.Description: This statement converts an 2D array of numeric values to a string separated by delimiters. The formatting of the string as well as the delimiter can be set.
Statement.Name: ArrayHandling_ArrayToSeparatedString2DDouble

Parameter.Name: ArrayName
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Name of the array to convert

Parameter.Name: Format string
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Format string to be used for converting single values to strings. E.g. use %d for decimal values, %f for floating point values. Refer to the LabVIEW help for more informations about how to use format strings

Parameter.Name: delimiter (Tab)
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select the delimiter to be used. Delimiters can be SPACE, TAB, COMMA or SEMICOLON

Parameter.Name: CommaSeparatedString
Parameter.Description: Result: String of separated values

Statement.Description: Calculate 1D double arrays using the given operation. If parameter "SecondArrayName" is used, all corresponding elements of "FirstArrayName" and "SecondArrayName" are calculated. If parameter "SecondArrayName" is empty, all elements of "FirstArrayName" are calculated according to the operation with skalar value "Value". The result is stored in "DestinationArrayName". All arrays must have the same dimension and size. Otherwise "Calculate OK?" will return "false"
Statement.Name: ArrayHandling_CalculateArray1DDouble

Parameter.Name: FirstArrayName
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Name of first array to calculate with

Parameter.Name: SecondArrayName
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Name of second array to calculate with. Use "* use constant *" to use the skalar value for calculating.

Parameter.Name: Operation
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Choose operation from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: DestinationArrayName
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Name of array to copy the result to

Parameter.Name: Value
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Skalar value to calculate with if parameter "SecondArrayName" is set to "* use constant *"

Parameter.Name: Calculate OK?
Parameter.Description: Result: "True" if operation was successful. "False" if sizes of arrays does not match or one or more array is not initialized.

Statement.Description: Calculate 2D double arrays using the given operation. If parameter "SecondArrayName" is used, all corresponding elements of "FirstArrayName" and "SecondArrayName" are calculated. If parameter "SecondArrayName" is empty, all elements of "FirstArrayName" are calculated according to the operation with skalar value "Value". The result is stored in "DestinationArrayName". All arrays must have the same dimension and size. Otherwise "Calculate OK?" will return "false"
Statement.Name: ArrayHandling_CalculateArray2DDouble

Parameter.Name: FirstArrayName
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Name of first array to calculate with

Parameter.Name: SecondArrayName
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Name of second array to calculate with. Use "* use constant *" to use the skalar value for calculating.

Parameter.Name: Operation
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Choose operation from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: DestinationArrayName
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Name of array to copy the result to

Parameter.Name: Value
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Skalar value to calculate with if parameter "SecondArrayName" is set to "* use constant *"

Parameter.Name: Calculate OK?
Parameter.Description: Result: "True" if operation was successful. "False" if sizes of arrays does not match or one or more array is not initialized.

Statement.Description: This statement gets a single numeric element from an 1D array currently loaded into memory.
Statement.Name: ArrayHandling_GetElement1DDouble

Parameter.Name: ArrayName
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Name of the array

Parameter.Name: Index
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Index in array to read the element from. Index starts with 0 (zero)

Parameter.Name: Element
Parameter.Description: Result: Value of element at index

Parameter.Name: Found?
Parameter.Description: Result: "True" if array found and index within bounds. "False" if array not found or index out of bounds.

Statement.Description: This statement gets a single numeric element from an 2D array currently loaded into memory.
Statement.Name: ArrayHandling_GetElement2DDouble

Parameter.Name: ArrayName
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Name of the array

Parameter.Name: Index Row
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Index in array to read the element from. Index starts with 0 (zero)

Parameter.Name: Index Column
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Index in array to read the element from. Index starts with 0 (zero)

Parameter.Name: Element
Parameter.Description: Result: Value of element at index

Parameter.Name: Found?
Parameter.Description: Result: "True" if array found and index within bounds. "False" if array not found or index out of bounds.

Statement.Description: Linearly interpolates a decimal y value from an array of numbers or points using a fractional index or x value.
Statement.Name: ArrayHandling_InterpolateArrayDouble

Parameter.Name: ArrayName
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Name of the array (2D)

Parameter.Name: FractionalIndex
Parameter.Description: Is the index or x-value at which the function should return a y-value.

Parameter.Name: ColumnIndex
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Column index in array. Index starts with 0 (zero)

Parameter.Name: ArrayFound?
Parameter.Description: Result: "True" if array found and index within bounds. "False" if array not found or index out of bounds.

Parameter.Name: InterpolatedElement
Parameter.Description: Result: is the interpolated value of the element at the fractional index or the interpolated y-value of the fractional data point,

Statement.Description: Convert a separated string containing numeric values to a 1D array of floating point values.
Statement.Name: ArrayHandling_SeparatedStringToArray1DDouble

Parameter.Name: ArrayName
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Name of the array that shall contain the numeric values converted from the separated string.

Parameter.Name: CommaSeparatedString
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Separted string to convert to array

Parameter.Name: delimiter (Tab)
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select the delimiter to be used. Delimiters can be SPACE, TAB, COMMA or SEMICOLON

Statement.Description: Convert a separated string containing numeric values to a 2D array of floating point values.
Statement.Name: ArrayHandling_SeparatedStringToArray2DDouble

Parameter.Name: ArrayName
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Name of the array that shall contain the numeric values converted from the separated string.

Parameter.Name: CommaSeparatedString
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Separted string to convert to array

Parameter.Name: delimiter (Tab)
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select the delimiter to be used. Delimiters can be SPACE, TAB, COMMA or SEMICOLON

Statement.Description: Set a single numeric element in an 1D array that is currently loaded into memory
Statement.Name: ArrayHandling_SetElement1DDouble

Parameter.Name: ArrayName
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Name of the array

Parameter.Name: Index
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Index in array to set the element to. Index starts with 0 (zero)

Parameter.Name: Element
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Value to write into array at index

Parameter.Name: Found?
Parameter.Description: Result: "True" if array found and index within bounds. "False" if array not found or index out of bounds.

Statement.Description: Set a single numeric element in an 2D array that is currently loaded into memory
Statement.Name: ArrayHandling_SetElement2DDouble

Parameter.Name: ArrayName
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Name of the array

Parameter.Name: Index Row
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Index in array to set the element to. Index starts with 0 (zero)

Parameter.Name: Element
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Value to write into array at index

Parameter.Name: Found?
Parameter.Description: Result: "True" if array found and index within bounds. "False" if array not found or index out of bounds.

Statement.Description: Sorts an 1D array either ascending or descending that is currently loaded into memory
Statement.Name: ArrayHandling_Sort1DDouble

Parameter.Name: ArrayName
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Name of the array

Parameter.Name: Descending?
Parameter.Description: Sorting direction is either ascending or descending.

Parameter.Name: Found?
Parameter.Description: Result: "True" if array found and index within bounds. "False" if array not found or index out of bounds.

Statement.Description: Sorts a 2D array either by the selected column or row index and either ascending or descending that is currently loaded into memory
Statement.Name: ArrayHandling_Sort2DDouble

Parameter.Name: ArrayName
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Name of the array

Parameter.Name: Descending?
Parameter.Description: Sorting direction is either ascending or descending.

Parameter.Name: Index
Parameter.Description: Specifies the index of the column or row with elements that you want to rearrange.

Parameter.Name: SortingDimension
Parameter.Description: Specifies the dimension of the 2D array to sort by.

Parameter.Name: Found?
Parameter.Description: Result: "True" if array found and index within bounds. "False" if array not found or index out of bounds.

Statement.Description: Interpolates points in a 1D array that represents a 2D non-descending graph. This function compares threshold y to the values in array of numbers or points starting at start index until it finds a pair of consecutive elements such that threshold y is greater than or equal to the value of the first element and less than or equal to the value of the second element.
Statement.Name: ArrayHandling_ThresholdArrayDouble

Parameter.Name: ArrayName
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Name of the array

Parameter.Name: ColumnIndex
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Column index in array. Index starts with 0 (zero)

Parameter.Name: Threshold
Parameter.Description: Is the threshold value for the function. If threshold y is less than or equal to the array value at start index, the function returns start index for fractional index or x. If threshold y is greater than every value in the array, the function returns the index of the last value. If the array is empty, the function returns NaN.

Parameter.Name: StartIndex
Parameter.Description: The default is 0, which means the function returns the result calculated from the entire array, rather than a specified section of the array.

Parameter.Name: FractionalIndex
Parameter.Description: Result: Value of element at index

Parameter.Name: ArrayFound?
Parameter.Description: Result: "True" if array found and index within bounds. "False" if array not found or index out of bounds.

Statement.Description: Add a new 1D string array to the array handling function. If an array with the given name already exists this statement will return "false" in the "Created" output
Statement.Name: ArrayHandling_AddNewArray1DString

Parameter.Name: ArrayName
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Name of the array to create

Parameter.Name: defaultValue
Parameter.Description: Parameter: All elements will be initialized with default value

Parameter.Name: dimension size
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Size of array in 1D

Parameter.Name: Created
Parameter.Description: Result: "True" if array was created. "False" if array was not created because it was already initialized with the same name.

Parameter.Name: ArrayName
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Name of the array

Parameter.Name: Descending?
Parameter.Description: Sorting direction is either ascending or descending.

Parameter.Name: Index
Parameter.Description: Specifies the index of the column or row with elements that you want to rearrange.

Parameter.Name: SortingDimension
Parameter.Description: Specifies the dimension of the 2D array to sort by.

Parameter.Name: Found?
Parameter.Description: Result: "True" if array found and index within bounds. "False" if array not found or index out of bounds.

Statement.Description: The axis selected in the Axes name parameter field will be calibrated to its mechanical center position. The axis must be fiducialized once before rooms can be set.
Statement.Name: FaultAck

Parameter.Name: Controller name
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select stage from drop down list.

Statement.Description:
Creates a file if not existing with an absolute path. If the file already exists, the user is gently asked if he wants to replace it. Return TRUE if done.
05.03.2014, CK, V1.0
Statement.Name: FileCreate

Statement.Description:
Checks if a file exists in an absolute path.
05.03.2014, CK, V1.0
Statement.Name: FileExists

Statement.Description:
Reads from an existing file (txt, csv, ...) with an absolute path. Returns a TRUE back if operation was done.
05.03.2014, CK, V1.0
Statement.Name: FileRead

Statement.Description:
Writes into a existing file (txt, csv, ...) with an absolute path. If the file is not existing an error occours. "continue" will generate this file. Returns a TRUE back if operation was done.
05.03.2014, CK, V1.0
Statement.Name: FileWrite

Statement.Description: This statement tries to locate an object using a defined set (see: EdgeFind panel).The results are the edge values of the largest object (area of connected pixel)found after processing the images defined by the Set. You can specify, whether the results should be returned in pixel coordinates or as calibrated values.
Statement.Name: FindObject_Extended

Parameter.Name: Camera
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select a camera from the drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Threshold Range Min
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select the minimum threshold.

Parameter.Name: Threshold Range Max
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select the maximum threshold.

Parameter.Name: Connectivity
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select the connectivity option.

Parameter.Name: Dilation
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Define the dilation parameter.

Parameter.Name: Erode
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Define the erode parameter.

Parameter.Name: Show picture
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Choose if picture will be shown: Show=activate show picture panel; Hide=Don?t show the picture.

Parameter.Name: result type
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select result type: calibrated of pixels.

Parameter.Name: ROI_left
Parameter.Description: Parameter: ROI coordinate: Left

Parameter.Name: ROI_top
Parameter.Description: Parameter: ROI coordinate: Top

Parameter.Name: ROI_right
Parameter.Description: Parameter: ROI coordinate: Right

Parameter.Name: ROI_bottom
Parameter.Description: Parameter: ROI coordinate: Bottom

Parameter.Name: Min X
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns minimum x position of found object.

Parameter.Name: Max X
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns maximum x position of found object.

Parameter.Name: Min Y
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns minimum y position of found object.

Parameter.Name: Max Y
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns maximum y position of found object.

Statement.Description: This statement converts the input X and Y coordinates using the camera calubration file, depending on the selection of the direction control, from pixel to calibration coordinates (PixelToCalibration) and from calibration to pixel coordinates (CalibrationToPixel). This statement will only work with a valid calibration file defined in the TestMaster, otherwise the in and output will be the same.
Statement.Name: IMAQ_ConvertCoordinates

Parameter.Name: Camera
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select a camera from the drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Direction
Parameter.Description: This defines in which direction the coordinate is converted.

Parameter.Name: Input.X
Parameter.Description: Input X coordinate in pixel or real world (depending on selection of direction)

Parameter.Name: Input.Y
Parameter.Description: Input Y coordinate in pixel or real world (depending on selection of direction)

Parameter.Name: Result.X
Parameter.Description: Resulting X coordinate after conversion.

Parameter.Name: Result.Y
Parameter.Description: Resulting Y coordinate after conversion.

Statement.Description: Locates a circular edge in a search area. This statement locates the intersection points between a set of search lines and the edge of an object.
Statement.Name: IMAQ_FindCircularEdge

Parameter.Name: Image In
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select camera from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: ROIDescriptorString
Parameter.Description: Parameter: ROI as flattened string

Parameter.Name: Edge Options.Edge Polarity
Parameter.Description: specifies the polarity of the edges to be found

Parameter.Name: Edge Options.Kernel Size
Parameter.Description: specifies the size of the edge detection kernel

Parameter.Name: Edge Options.Width
Parameter.Description: specifies the number of pixels averaged perpendicular to the search direction to compute the edge profile strength at each point along the search ROI

Parameter.Name: Edge Options.Minimum Edge Strength
Parameter.Description: specifies the minimum edge strength (gradient magnitude) required for a detected edge

Parameter.Name: Edge Options.Interpolation Type
Parameter.Description: specifies the interpolation method used to extract the data from the image that is used for detecting edges

Parameter.Name: Edge Options.Data Processing Method
Parameter.Description: the method used to process the data extracted for edge detection

Parameter.Name: ShowPicture?
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Choose if the picture is shown after process: Show = activate show picture panel ; Hide = Don?t show the picture.

Parameter.Name: Found
Parameter.Description: Result: A green light indicates, when focus is found: 0=light is not found; 1=focus is found; green light.

Statement.Description: This statement runs a pattern matching process with the given pattern on the given image and returns the x- and y-position as well as a matching score of the matched pattern. The position is corresponding to the middle of the pattern.
Statement.Name: IMAQ_MatchPatternRotational

Parameter.Name: Image In
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select camera from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Minimum Match Score
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter a value between 1 and 1000 for the minimum match score.

Parameter.Name: Show picture
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Choose if picture will be shown: Show=activate show picture panel; Hide=Don?t show the picture.

Statement.Description: This statement runs a shape matching process within a before defined ROI of the input picture
Statement.Name: IMAQDetectCirles

Parameter.Name: Image In
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select camera from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Minimum match score
Parameter.Description: Minimum match score is the minimum score a returned shape can have.

Parameter.Name: ROI left
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter left position of ROI.

Parameter.Name: ROI top
Parameter.Description: Parameter. Enter top position of ROI.

Parameter.Name: ROI right
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter right position of ROI.

Parameter.Name: ROI bottom
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter bottom position of ROI.

Parameter.Name: ShowPicture?
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Choose if the picture is shown after process: Show = activate show picture panel ; Hide = Don?t show the picture.

Parameter.Name: Found
Parameter.Description: Result: A green light indicates, when focus is found: 0=light is not found; 1=focus is found; green light.

Parameter.Name: PositionX
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns x position of found pattern.

Parameter.Name: PositionY
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns y position of found pattern.

Parameter.Name: Score
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns score value.

Parameter.Name: Radius
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns rotation angle between pattern and found object.

Statement.Description:
Copies an existing array by using the multiplier as a transfer operation on each value. If the sink array does not exist, it is created internally (otherwise values are attached!). Use ReInitialize option 'true' to ensure, the ArraySink will be disposed prior to creation.
v.1.1 FR
Statement.Name: Array_Copy

Statement.Description:
Creates a new array. Choose ReInitialize 'true' option to ensure the new array to be empty (in case there is already an existing array with the same name).
v.1.1 FR
Statement.Name: Array_Create

Statement.Description:
Deletes a value in an existing array.
v.1.0 FR
Statement.Name: Array_DeleteValue

Statement.Description:
Displays the content of an array.
v.1.0 FR
Statement.Name: Array_DisplayData

Statement.Description:
Displays the content of two arrays in one graph.
v.1.0 FR
Statement.Name: Array_DisplayData2D

Statement.Description:
Displays the content of two arrays (XY graph).
v.1.1 FR
Statement.Name: Array_DisplayDataXY

Statement.Description:
Disposes (deletes) an existing array.
v.1.0 FR
Statement.Name: Array_Dispose

Statement.Description:
Exports the content of an array to a given file. First two entries are reserved for date/time and optional commenting purpose.
v.1.1 FR
Statement.Name: Array_Export

Statement.Description:
Performs a fit function of two given input arrays. Options are: Linear Fit, Exponential Fit, Power Fit, Gaussan Fit, Logarithm Fit or Polynomial Fit. Array X can be emtpy. In this case, the calculation will be performed in respect to the array indexing. ReturnParam1: slope, amplitude, amplitude, amplitude, amplitude, - ReturnParam2: intercept, damping, power, center, scale, - ReturnParam3: - , - , - , standard deviation, - , - Residue: MeanSquareError ReturnArray will contain the resulting best fit function. This array will be (re-)initialized prior to use, any old data will be lost.
v.1.3 FR
Statement.Name: Array_FitFunction

Parameter.Name: ReturnParam1
Parameter.Description: residue returns the weighted mean error of the fitted model. If method is Least Absolute Residual, residue is the weighted mean absolute error. Otherwise, residue is the weighted mean square error.

Parameter.Name: ReturnParam2
Parameter.Description: variance is the calculated variance of the values in the input sequence X.

Parameter.Name: ReturnParam3
Parameter.Description: variance is the calculated variance of the values in the input sequence X.

Parameter.Name: Residue
Parameter.Description: residue returns the weighted mean error of the fitted model. If method is Least Absolute Residual, residue is the weighted mean absolute error. Otherwise, residue is the weighted mean square error.

Statement.Description:
Imports a specific set of data (given by SelectedRow, starting with line 1-n) into an array. The array will be (re-)initialized. Prior data will be lost. If SelectedRow is 0, the last line is imported. The length of the imported data is determined by the longest set of data (shorter rows will be padded with 0's!). First two entries in each row are reserved for date/time and comment purpose and will be not imported.
v.1.2 FR
Statement.Name: Array_Import

Parameter.Name: SelectedRow
Parameter.Description: number of rows is the maximum number of rows or lines the VI reads.

Statement.Description:
Inserts a value in an existing array.
v.1.0 FR
Statement.Name: Array_InsertValue

Statement.Description:
This statement calculates a linear interpolation between two indexes. The input array (starting with index 1) can either be the name of an existing array or directly transfered as a list (comma separated) of numbers. It can be selected to obtain either a value @ any given (fractional) index or the index @ any given threshold. The second option will return a comma separated list at 'ValueString' and the number of found values at 'Value'.
v.1.0 FR
Statement.Name: Array_Interpolation

Parameter.Name: Value
Parameter.Description: variance is the calculated variance of the values in the input sequence X.

Statement.Description:
Perfoms a mathematical operation to all values inside an array (matching the given condition). The new values are stored inside the very same array. Old values are overwritten. It can be choosen to either keep or remove not matching values.
v.1.1 FR
Statement.Name: Array_MathOperation

Statement.Description:
Reads back a value. Options are: Min, Max, Mean, All, Index, Range, Count
v.1.0 FR
Statement.Name: Array_ReadValue

Statement.Description:
Sorts all values in an existing array. Options are descending or ascending order.
v.1.0 FR
Statement.Name: Array_Sort

Statement.Description:
Performs a statistical analysis of a given input array. The input can either be a predefined array (identified by its name) or a comma separated list of values.
v.1.1 FR
Statement.Name: Array_Statistics

Parameter.Name: ReturnValue
Parameter.Description: variance is the calculated variance of the values in the input sequence X.

Statement.Description:
Returns the larger or smaller element of two input numbers.
v. 1.0 FR

Statement.Description:
Math-Functions: addition, subtraction, multiplication, dividation, modulo, power, square root, log n, log 10, round, signum, sin, arcsin, cos, arccos, tan, arctan, absolute, invertion.
Please pay attention to following functions: --> power: op1 = base, op2 = exp --> sqrt, log n, log 10, sign, inv, abs, sin, asin, cos, acos, tan, atan: only op1 is used (op2 is ignored) --> round: op1 = floating point number, op2 is amount of numbers after comma v.1.0 CK
Statement.Name: Math

Statement.Description: This statement deletes the data file specified by file name within data directory.
Statement.Name: DeleteDataFile

Parameter.Name: File name
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Name of the data file to be deleted from data directory.

Statement.Description: Deletes the data file specified by the file name at a specified location in the file system. The data file can be located in the process, data, log or user path of the software environment or at any other location which can be specified by selecting Path as BasePath and specifying an absolute path in the Folder field.
Statement.Name: DeleteDataFilewithPath

Parameter.Name: File name
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Name of data file to be deleted.

Parameter.Name: base Path
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select a base path from the drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Folder
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Give the absolute path to the folder where the data file to be deleted is located.

Statement.Description: Creates and opens a new data file which will be used by the subsequent Data file-statements. The file is named NamePrefixyyyymmddhhmmss.dat. The file is saved in the Data-path. The generation time of the file is entered in the first data line. The DataFile remains valid until the software is restarted or a new OpenDataFile is performed.
Statement.Name: OpenDataFile

Parameter.Name: File name prefix
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter a file name prefix.

Statement.Description: Reads the contents of the file defined by File name..
Statement.Name: ReadDataFileString

Parameter.Name: File name
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter name of file to be read.

Parameter.Name: String
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns data file string.

Statement.Description: Reads the specified line from a data file and returns up to 6 values as strings. The data file has to be located within the software?s data folder. As delimiter comma, tab and blank are available.

Parameter.Name: File name
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Name of data file within the softwares data folder.

Parameter.Name: Linenumber
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Specify the line to be read from the data file.

Parameter.Name: Delimiter
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select a delimiter from the drop down list. Available are comma, tab or blank.

Parameter.Description: Result: Returns first value in line as string.

Parameter.Name: String 2
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns second value in line as string.

Parameter.Name: String 3
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns third value in line as string.

Parameter.Name: String 4
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns fourth value in line as string.

Parameter.Name: String 5
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns fifth value in line as string.

Parameter.Name: String 6
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns sixth value in line as string.

Statement.Description: Reads the specified line from a data file and returns up to 6 values as strings. The data file can be located in the process, data, log or user path of the software environment or at any other location which can be specified by selecting Path as BasePath and specifying an absolute path in the Folder field. As delimiter comma, tab and blank are available.

Statement.Description: Allows saving up to three numerical parameters with three corresponding name-strings as a new line to a tab delimited ASCII-file, which has before been opened by OpenDataFile. The line will start with its time stamp of generation.

Parameter.Description: Parameter: Name of first value to be stored.

Parameter.Name: Value 1
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Numerical value to be stored.

Parameter.Name: Data name 2
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Name of second value to be stored.

Statement.Description: This statement appends a relative path to the base path.

Parameter.Name: Base Path
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter base path.

Parameter.Name: Append
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Relative path to be appended to the base path.

Parameter.Name: Path out
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns the complete path.

Statement.Description: This statement lists all files and folders of the by path specified folder.
Statement.Name: CheckIfFileExists

Parameter.Name: file path
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Path to the folder contents to be listed.

Parameter.Name: Exists?
Parameter.Description: Result: Path exists=true; no path=false

Statement.Description: Creates the directory specified by directory path. If a file or directory already exists at the specified location, this function returns an error instead of overwriting the existing file or directory.
Statement.Name: CreateDirectory

Parameter.Name: directory path
Parameter.Description: Statement: Path of the directory to be created.

Statement.Description: Copies the file or directory specified by source path to the location specified by target path.
Statement.Name: FileCopy

Parameter.Name: source path
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Source path which specifies the file or directory to be copied.

Parameter.Name: target path
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Target path is the new path, including the new file or directory name, for the file or directory you want to move. If a file already exists at target path, this function returns an error, if [overwrite=false). If a directory exists at target path, this function tries to copy the file or directory at source path to the location specified by appending the file or directory name (that is, the last element) of source path to target path. If a file or directory exists at this location, this function returns an error, if [overwrite=false). This function also returns an error if the target path is an invalid path (for example, when the specified parent directory is not valid).

Parameter.Name: overwrite
Parameter.Description: Parameter: applies if target file already exists. If False the existing file is not overwritten (Success=False). If True the existing target file will be overwritten (Success = True)

Parameter.Name: success
Parameter.Description: Result: indicates, if the operation was successful

Statement.Description: Copies the directory specified by source path to the location specified by target path. File pattern can be appiled to filter the files. This function doesen't work recursivelly on subdirectories. So only the files in the source path are copied, not any subdirectory of [Source path].
Statement.Name: FileCopyWithPattern

Parameter.Name: source path
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Source path which specifies the file or directory to be copied.

Parameter.Name: target path
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Target path is the new path, including the new file or directory name, for the file or directory you want to move. If a file already exists at target path, this function returns an error, if [overwrite=false). If a directory exists at target path, this function tries to copy the file or directory at source path to the location specified by appending the file or directory name (that is, the last element) of source path to target path. If a file or directory exists at this location, this function returns an error, if [overwrite=false). This function also returns an error if the target path is an invalid path (for example, when the specified parent directory is not valid).

Parameter.Name: pattern(only if folder copy, empty=all)
Parameter.Description: Parameter: If folder is copied this parameter defines the file pattern to copy. This parameter has no function, if only a file is copied. Default is all (*.*).

Parameter.Name: overwrite
Parameter.Description: Parameter: applies if target file already exists. If False the existing file is not overwritten (Success=False). If True the existing target file will be overwritten (Success = True)

Parameter.Name: success
Parameter.Description: Result: indicates, if the operation was successful

Statement.Description: This statement deletes the file or directory specified by path.
Statement.Name: FileDelete

Parameter.Name: mode
Parameter.Description: Paremeter: specifies if only files or the entire hierarchy should be deleted the the denoted location

Parameter.Name: path
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Specifies the file or directory to be deleted.

Parameter.Name: success
Parameter.Description: Result: indicates, if the operation was successful

Statement.Description: Deletes files within the [folder path]. File pattern can be appiled to filter the files. This function doesen't work recursivelly on subdirectories. So only the files in the source path are deleted, not any subdirectory of [folder path].
Statement.Name: FileDeleteWithPattern

Parameter.Name: folder path
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Source path which specifies the file or directory to be deleted.

Parameter.Name: file pattern
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Defines the file pattern to delete files. Default is all (*.*).

Parameter.Name: success
Parameter.Description: Result: indicates, if the operation was successful

Statement.Description: Moves the file or directory specified by source path to the location specified by target path.
Statement.Name: FileMove

Parameter.Name: source path
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Source path is the path to the file or directory to be moved.

Parameter.Name: target path
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Target path is the new path, including the new file or directory name, for the file or directory you want to move. If a file already exists at target path, this function returns an error, if [overwrite=false). If a directory exists at target path, this function tries to move the file or directory at source path to the location specified by appending the file or directory name (that is, the last element) of source path to target path. If a file or directory exists at this location, this function returns an error, if [overwrite=false). This function also returns an error if the target path is an invalid path (for example, when the specified parent directory is not valid).

Parameter.Name: overwrite
Parameter.Description: Parameter: applies if target file already exists. If False the existing file is not overwritten (Success=False). If True the existing target file will be overwritten (Success = True)

Statement.Description: This statement deletes all files in the given directory.
Statement.Name: FilesInFolderDelete

Parameter.Name: path
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Path to the directory that contains the files to be deleted.

Parameter.Name: success
Parameter.Description: Result: indicates, if the operation was successful

Statement.Description: Returns the file extension, without the period (.), of a file you specify.
Statement.Name: GetFileExtension

Parameter.Name: source path
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Source path is the path to the file or directory to be moved.

Parameter.Name: filename_wo_ext
Parameter.Description: filename without extension returns the name of the file you specify in file without the directory path, period (.), or file extension. Unlike lowercase file extension, filename without extension matches the case of the original filename.

Parameter.Name: file_extension
Parameter.Description: lowercase file extension returns the extension of the file, without the period (.). LabVIEW always returns a lowercase file extension, even if file contains uppercase letters.

Parameter.Name: Path_wo_extension
Parameter.Description: filename without extension returns the name of the file you specify in file with the directory path but without period (.), or file extension. Unlike lowercase file extension, filename without extension matches the case of the original filename.

Statement.Description: This statement returns the actual size and timestamp of the given directory or file
Statement.Name: GetTimeStampAndSize

Parameter.Name: Timestamp
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns current time stamp of file.

Statement.Description: This statement lists all files and folders of the by path specified folder.
Statement.Name: ListFolderContent

Parameter.Name: path
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Path to the folder contents to be listed.

Parameter.Name: Exists?
Parameter.Description: Result: Path exists=true; no path=false

Parameter.Name: NumberOfFilesInFolder
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns number of files in the selected folder.

Parameter.Name: ListOfFiles
Parameter.Description: Result: Comma separated list of files in the path.

Parameter.Name: NumberOfFoldersInFolder
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns the number of folders in the path.

Parameter.Name: ListOfFolders
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns the number of folders in the path.

Statement.Description: Returns two arrays of strings listing the names of all files and folders found in path, filtering both arrays based upon pattern and filtering the filenames array based upon the specified datalog type.
Statement.Name: ListFolderContentwithPattern

Parameter.Name: path
Parameter.Description: path identifies the folder whose contents you want to determine. If this is not an existing folder, this function sets filenames and folder names to empty arrays and returns an error. If path points to a VI library (*.llb), filenames returns the contents of the VI library and folder names returns an empty array.

Parameter.Name: pattern
Parameter.Description: pattern restricts the files and directories returned to those whose names match pattern. The pattern matching in this function is similar to the matching used in matching wildcards in Windows and Linux filenames and is not like the regular expression matching performed by the Match Pattern function and the Match Regular Expression function. If you specify characters other than the question mark character (?) or the asterisk character (*), the function displays only files or directories that contain those characters. You can use the question mark character (?) to match any single character. You can use the asterisk character (*) to match any sequence of zero or more characters.

Parameter.Name: Exists?
Parameter.Description: Result: Path exists=true; no path=false

Parameter.Name: NumberOfFilesInFolder
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns number of files in the selected folder.

Parameter.Name: ListOfFiles
Parameter.Description: filenames contains the names of the files found in the specified directory. This function does not return the names of files found in folders within the directory. This function sorts the returned filenames alphabetically.

Parameter.Name: NumberOfFoldersInFolder
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns the number of folders in the path.

Parameter.Name: ListOfFolders
Parameter.Description: folder names contains the names of the folders found in the specified directory. This function sorts the folder names alphabetically. If path is an empty path, folder names contains the names of the drives on the computer.

Statement.Description: Lists the contents of a folder or LLB. In order for this VI to work correctly, the LLB must have a .llb file extension.
Statement.Name: ListFolderContentwithPatternRecursive

Parameter.Name: path
Parameter.Description: Folder Path specifies the path to the folder or LLB whose contents you want LabVIEW to return.

Parameter.Name: pattern
Parameter.Description: pattern specifies the pattern for files for which you want to search in Folder Path. You can use the question mark character (?) to match any single character. You can use the asterisk character (*) to match any sequence of one or more characters. For example, if you want this VI to find a list of the VIs in a folder, specify *.vi for pattern.

Parameter.Name: ContinueOnError
Parameter.Description: Continue Recursing on Error? specifies whether to continue recursing directories if an error occurs. If Continue Recursing on Error? is TRUE, the VI continues recursing directory contents after the error occurs. If Continue Recursing on Error? is FALSE, the VI stops recursing directory content when the error occurs.

Parameter.Name: LLBsasFolders
Parameter.Description: Treat LLBs as Folders? specifies whether to list LLBs as files or folders. If Treat LLBs as Folders? is TRUE, this VI includes LLBs in the list of folders. If Treat LLBs as Folders? is FALSE, this VI includes LLBs in the list of files. The default is FALSE. If Folder Path specifies the path to an LLB, this VI ignores this input and returns all files, including LLBs, in All Files in Dir.

Parameter.Name: excludeFolders
Parameter.Description: Folders to Exclude specifies folder names you want to exclude during the recursion. Any folder you exclude does not appear in the All Folders array. This input is case insensitive. Multiple folders can be defined by using a colon as separator.

Parameter.Name: Exists?
Parameter.Description: Result: Path exists=true; no path=false

Parameter.Name: NumberOfFiles
Parameter.Description: Number of Files returns the number of files in the All Files in Dir array.

Parameter.Name: AllFilesInDir
Parameter.Description: All Files in Dir returns a list of all files in Folder Path. If Treat LLBs as Folders? is set to TRUE, the list of files includes LLBs. The list also includes shortcuts to files.

Parameter.Name: AllFolders
Parameter.Description: All Folders returns a list of all subfolders, and shortcuts to folders in Folder Path.

Statement.Description:
This statement waits for a file to be changed or createds Change is evaluated by comparing the timestamp of file to change to a previously read out timestamp. Get previous timestamp by statement "GetTimeStamp".
If file is not already present this statement returns if the file is created or a timeout occurs.
Statement.Name: WaitForFileChangedOrCreated

Parameter.Name: Last timestamp
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Previous timestamp of the file. Read it with statement "GetTimestamp" first.

Parameter.Name: File or directory path
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Filepath to check for a new timestamp

Parameter.Name: Timeout [ms]
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Time in [ms] to wait for changed file.

Parameter.Name: Timeout
Parameter.Description: Result: Timeout occurred

Parameter.Name: Timestamp
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns current time stamp of file.

Statement.Description: Writes data to a file.
Statement.Name: WriteToFile

Parameter.Name: File Path
Parameter.Description: file path is the path name of the file. If file path is empty (default) or is , the VI displays a dialog box from which you can select a file. Error 43 occurs if you cancel the dialog box.

Parameter.Name: Mode
Parameter.Description: Parameter: select if file should be created or replaced (New File), or the data should be appended to an existing file.

Parameter.Name: Data
Parameter.Description: Data to be written to a file.

Parameter.Name: success
Parameter.Description: Result: indicates, if the operation was successful

Statement.Description: Performs an Auto Focus operation using a defined set (see: AutoFocus panel). If **** is chosen as setup, the parameters entered in the Sequence Editor are activated. Additionally you may specify a different ROI. The statement returns to the final position, as well as an indication if an optimum focus could be found in the given range or not.
Statement.Name: AutoFocus

Parameter.Name: Setup
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Activates parameters enter ed in the sequence by ****. If a Parameter Set is selected from the drop down list, the chosen parameter set is activated.

Parameter.Name: Camera
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select a camera from the drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Axis
Parameter.Description: Parameter:Select axis from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: ROI left
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Define left position of ROI.

Parameter.Name: ROI top
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Define top position of ROI.

Parameter.Name: ROI right
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Define right position of ROI.

Parameter.Name: ROI bottom
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Define bottom position of ROI.

Parameter.Name: Show pic?
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Choose if picture will be shown: Show=activate show picture panel. Hide=Don?t show the picture.

Parameter.Name: Found?
Parameter.Description: Result: A green light indicates, when focus is found: 0=light is not found; 1=focus is found; green light.

Parameter.Name: Z pos
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns final z-position of focus.

Statement.Description: Determines an AutoFocus by finding the absolute maximum value in the histograms taken in the scan range.

Parameter.Name: Setup
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Activates parameters enter ed in the sequence by****. If a Parameter Set is selected from the drop down list, the chosen parameter set is activated.

Parameter.Name: Camera
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select a camera from the drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Axis
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select axis from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Scanlength (µm)
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter scan length in µm.

Parameter.Name: # of steps
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter number of camera stops.

Parameter.Name: Show pic?
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Choose if picture will be shown: Show=activate show picture panel; Hide=Don?t show the picture.

Parameter.Name: Found?
Parameter.Description: Result: A green light indicates, when focus is found: 0=light is not found; 1=focus is found; green light.

Parameter.Name: Z pos
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns final z-position of focus.

Statement.Description: This statement detects an edge in the given image by using these parameters in the panel .Here always the most Left or most Top Edge is returned depending on the set ROI.
Statement.Name: DetectEdge

Parameter.Name: Parameters
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Activate parameters entered in the sequence by ****. If a Parameter Set is selected from the drop down list, the chosen parameter set is activated.

Parameter.Name: result type
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select result type from drop down list: Calibrated or pixels.

Parameter.Name: Show picture
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Choose if picture will be shown: Show=activate show picture panel; Hide=Don?t show the picture.

Parameter.Name: X
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns x position of detected edge.

Parameter.Name: Y
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns y position of detected edge.

Statement.Description: Statement used for edge detection on an camera picture. Either use a predefined set by selecting the corresponding set from the "Parameter" Value or you can choose to define the parameters manually by selecting the "Parameter" set "****" This statement allows defining a search direction. Therefore it is possible to define the Edge Side from which the search is started. The first found edge is returned.
Statement.Name: DetectEdge2

Parameter.Name: Parameters
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Activate parameters entered in the sequence by ****. If a Parameter Set is selected from the drop down list, the chosen parameter set is activated.

Parameter.Name: result type
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select result type from drop down list: Calibrated or pixels.

Parameter.Name: Show picture
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Choose if picture will be shown: Show=activate show picture panel; Hide=Don?t show the picture.

Parameter.Name: Camera
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Choose the camera from the list, which should be used for edge detection.

Parameter.Name: Level Type
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Choose which LevelType should be used for edge detection [Absolute Value; Relative Value]

Parameter.Name: ThresholdLevel
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Define the Threshold Level that should be used for edge detection.

Parameter.Name: Hysteresis
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Define the Hysteresis that should be used for edge detection.

Parameter.Name: x1
Parameter.Description: Parameter: The x1 coordinate of the line used for detecting an edge.

Parameter.Name: y1
Parameter.Description: Parameter: The y1 coordinate of the line used for detecting an edge.

Parameter.Name: x2
Parameter.Description: Parameter: The x2 coordinate of the line used for detecting an edge.

Parameter.Name: y2
Parameter.Description: Parameter: The y2 coordinate of the line used for detecting an edge.

Parameter.Name: EdgeSide
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Choose search direction that should be used [Left/Top;Right/Bottom]

Parameter.Name: X
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns x position of detected edge.

Parameter.Name: Y
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns y position of detected edge.

Statement.Description: This statement performs an autofocusation by determining the shift of the edge during focusation.
Statement.Name: EdgeDetectAutoFocus

Parameter.Name: Setup
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Activates parameters enter ed in the sequence by****. If a Parameter Set is selected from the drop down list, the chosen parameter set is activated.

Parameter.Name: Camera
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select a camera from the drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Axis
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select axis from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Scanlength (µm)
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter scan length in µm.

Parameter.Name: # of steps
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter number of camera stops.

Parameter.Name: ROI x1
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Define x1 position of ROI.

Parameter.Name: ROI y1
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Define y1 position of ROI.

Parameter.Name: ROI x2
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Define x2 position of ROI.

Parameter.Name: ROI y2
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Define Y2 position of ROI.

Parameter.Name: Show pic?
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Choose if the picture is shown after process: Show=activate show picture panel; Hide=Don?t show the picture.

Parameter.Name: Threshold Parameters.Level Typey
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select if threshold level is expressed in absolute or relative values. Default: absolute.

Parameter.Name: Threshold Parameters.Threshold Level
Parameter.Description: Parameter: If threshold level is absolute: threshold is given on basis of pixel values. If threshold level is relative, threshold is expressed as percentage of the pixel-value range found along the path defined by the pixel coordinates.

Parameter.Name: Threshold Parameters.Hysteresis
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Difference in threshold level between a rising and a falling edge, enabling accurate detection in noisy images.

Parameter.Name: EdgeSide
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Specifies to start the search for the edge from the given directions. First found edge is taken for evaluation.

Parameter.Name: Found?
Parameter.Description: Result: A green light indicates, when focus is found: 0=light is not found; 1=focus is found; green light.

Parameter.Name: Z pos
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns final z-position of edge.

Parameter.Name: ROI bottom
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter bottom position of ROI.

Parameter.Name: Show pic?
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Choose if the picture is shown after process: Show=activate show picture panel; Hide=Don?t show the picture.

Parameter.Name: Found?

Statement.Description: This statement tries to locate an object using a defined set (see: EdgeFind panel).The results are the edge values of the largest object (area of connected pixel)found after processing the images defined by the Set. You can specify, whether the results should be returned in pixel coordinates or as calibrated values.
Statement.Name: FindObject

Parameter.Name: Camera
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select a camera from the drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Parameters
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Activate parameters entered in the sequence by ****. If a Parameter Set is selected from the drop down list, the chosen parameter set is activated.

Parameter.Name: Show picture
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Choose if picture will be shown: Show=activate show picture panel; Hide=Don?t show the picture.

Parameter.Name: result type
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select result type: calibrated of pixels.

Parameter.Name: Min X
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns minimum x position of found object.

Parameter.Name: Max X
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns maximum x position of found object.

Parameter.Name: Min Y
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns minimum y position of found object.

Parameter.Name: Max Y

Statement.Description: This statement runs a pattern matching process and returns x and y position as well a matching score of the matched pattern. The final position is corresponding to the middle of the pattern.
Statement.Name: MatchPattern

Parameter.Name: Camera
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select a camera from the drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Pattern
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select a pattern from the drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Minimum Match Score
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter a value between 1 and 1000 for the minimum match score.

Parameter.Name: Found
Parameter.Description: Result: A green light indicates, when focus is found: 0=light is not found; 1=focus is found; green light.

Parameter.Name: PositionX
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns x position of found pattern.

Parameter.Name: PositionY
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns y position of found pattern.

Parameter.Name: Score
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns score value.

Statement.Description: This statement runs a pattern matching process within a before defined ROI of the grabbed live picture; also turned patterns and patterns of a different size are found. Retrieved values are the x and y position, the matching score of the process, the size of the object and the rotation angle.
Statement.Name: MatchPatternROI

Parameter.Name: Camera
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select a camera from the drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Pattern
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select a pattern from the drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Minimum Match Score
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter a value between 1 and 1000 for the minimum match score.

Parameter.Name: ROI left
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter left position of ROI.

Parameter.Name: ROI top
Parameter.Description: Parameter. Enter top position of ROI.

Parameter.Name: ROI right
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter right position of ROI.

Parameter.Name: ROI bottom
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter bottom position of ROI.

Parameter.Name: ShowPicture?
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Choose if the picture is shown after process: Show = activate show picture panel ; Hide = Don?t show the picture.

Parameter.Name: Found
Parameter.Description: Result: A green light indicates, when focus is found: 0=light is not found; 1=focus is found; green light.

Parameter.Name: PositionX
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns x position of found pattern.

Parameter.Name: PositionY
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns y position of found pattern.

Parameter.Name: Score
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns score value.

Parameter.Name: SizeX
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns x size of found object.

Parameter.Name: SizeY
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns y size of found object.

Parameter.Name: Angle
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns rotation angle between pattern and found object.

Statement.Description: This statement runs a pattern matching process; also turned patterns are found. Retrieved values are the rotation angle, the x and y position as well as the matching score of the process.
Statement.Name: MatchPatternWithAngle

Parameter.Name: Camera
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select a camera from the drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Pattern
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select a pattern from the drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Minimum Match Score
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter a value between 1 and 1000 for the minimum match score.

Parameter.Name: Match Mode
Parameter.Description: Match Mode specifies the technique to use when looking for the template pattern in the image.

Parameter.Name: Found
Parameter.Description: Result: A green light indicates, when focus is found: 0=light is not found; 1=focus is found; green light.

Parameter.Name: Score
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns score value.

Parameter.Name: PositionX
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns x position of found pattern.

Parameter.Name: PositionY
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns y position of found pattern.

Parameter.Name: Angle
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns rotation angle between pattern and found object.

Statement.Description: The Rake Edge command finds edges along a set of parallel lines defined inside a rectangular region. Edges are determined based on their contrast and slope .A linear fit is applied to all found edge points. The points with the largest distance from the resulting edge line are reduced until the mean square error of the resulting line is smaller than the defined Max MSE.
Statement.Name: RakeEdge

Parameter.Name: Setup
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Activates parameters enter ed in the sequence by ****. If a Parameter Set is selected from the drop down list, the chosen parameter set is activated.

Parameter.Name: Camera
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select a camera from the drop down list.

Parameter.Name: ROILeft
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter left position of ROI.

Parameter.Name: ROITop
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter top position of ROI.

Parameter.Name: ROIRight
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter right position of ROI.

Parameter.Name: ROIBottom
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter bottom position of ROI.

Parameter.Name: Contrast
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Contrast specifies the threshold for the contrast of the edge. Only edges with a contrast greater than this value are used in the detection process. Contrast is defined as the difference between the average pixel intensity before the edge and the average pixel intensity after the edge.

Parameter.Name: Filter width
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Filter width specifies the number of pixels that are averaged to find the contrast at either side of the edge.

Parameter.Name: Steepness
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Steepness specifies the slope of the edge. This value represents the number of pixels that correspond to the transition area of the edge.

Parameter.Name: Step Size
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Step Size defines the distance, in pixels, between the parallel lines inside the rectangular region.

Parameter.Name: Scan Direction
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Scan Direction defines the direction along which edges are searched for along the lines. It can be either Left to Right, Right to Left, Top to Bottom, or Bottom to Top.
Parameter.EnumStrings: Bottom to Top,Left to Right,Right to Left,Top to Bottom

Parameter.Name: MaxMSE
Parameter.Description: Parameter: A linear fit is applied to all found edge points. The points with the largest distance from the resulting edge line are reduced until the mean square error of the resulting line is smaller than the defined Max MSE.

Parameter.Name: points used
Parameter.Description: Result: The numbers of points, which are used for the linear fit of the edge line, are returned.

Parameter.Name: Edge
Parameter.Description: Result:This parameter returns the coordinate of the detected edge.

Statement.Description: This statement sets the zoom factor for the image displayed in the given window number. Select a positive factor to display the image larger than its size and a negative factor to display the image smaller than its size. If the image window is smaller than the displayed image the position of the image that is centered in the window can be specified.

Parameter.Description: Parameter: Message to be displayed to the user in the window title.

Parameter.Name: Tool
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select the tool to activate

Parameter.Name: ROIDescriptorIn
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Optionally input a ROI to be displayed to the user when the dialog starts.

Parameter.Name: ImageIn
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Image to be displayed.

Parameter.Name: ROIDescriptorOut
Parameter.Description: Result: ROI as a flattened string to be used in YASE variables.

Parameter.Name: Cancel
Parameter.Description: Result: Indicates if the user canceled the operation.
