Parameter.Description: Parameter: Choose analog line from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Value
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns the value: 0 equals FALSE, 1 equals TRUE.

Parameter.Name: LastChangeTime
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns time, the last change has been performed.

Statement.Description: This statement sets or resets the selected digital line.
Statement.Name: SetDigOut

Parameter.Name: Digital Line
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select digital line from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: State
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select on or off.

Parameter.Name: LastChangeTime
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns time, the last change has been performed.

Statement.Description: This statement sets an output to a discrete (integer) value.
Statement.Name: SetDiscreteOut

Parameter.Name: Discrete parameter
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select a discrete parameter from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Value
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter a discrete value.

Parameter.Name: LastChangeTime
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns time, the last change has been performed.

Statement.Description: This statement sets the value of an output IOPoint defined with enumerated values
Statement.Name: SetEnum

Parameter.Name: IOPoint
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Choose enum type IOPoint from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: State
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select the enum state to be applied. (Dependend on selected IOPoint

Parameter.Name: LastChangeTime
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns time, the last change has been performed.

Statement.Description: This statement sets an IOPoint value by name. Only analog, discrete, and binary IOPoints are supported.
Statement.Name: SetIOPoint

Parameter.Name: IO Point Name
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select digital line from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Value
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter value to set.

Parameter.Name: LastChangeTime
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns time, the last change has been performed.

Statement.Description: This statement reads the input value of the selected IOPoint line and returns its value.
Statement.Name: SetIOPointProperty

Parameter.Name: IOPoint Name
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select analog line from drop down list.

Statement.Description: Sets an output to a discrete (signed integer) value.
Statement.Name: SetSignedDiscreteOut

Parameter.Name: Discrete parameter
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select IOPoint from list.

Parameter.Name: Value
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter the discrete value to be set.

Parameter.Name: LastChangeTime
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns time, the last change has been performed.

Statement.Description: This statement sets an output to a string value.
Statement.Name: SetStringOut

Parameter.Name: String Line
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select string line from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: String
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Set string value.

Parameter.Name: LastChangeTime
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns time, the last change has been performed.

Statement.Description: Sets value of IOPoint to off = 1 or on = 0.
Statement.Name: SetValve

Parameter.Name: Valve
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Name of IOPoint to be set. Supported IOPoint types: analog, discrete, binary.

Parameter.Name: State
Parameter.Description: Parameter: State of IOPoint to set (off 0 1, on = 0)

Parameter.Name: LastChangeTime
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns time, the last change has been performed.

Statement.Description: Waits for a digital line to switch to a specific state. Warning: This function should only be used in single-sequencer setups, otherwise sequences running in parallel will be interrupted until WaitDigIn has finished.
Statement.Name: WaitDigIn

Parameter.Name: Digital Line
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select digital line from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: State
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Waits for state: on or off.

Parameter.Name: Timeout (ms)
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter timeout in ms.

Parameter.Name: Timeout
Parameter.Description: Result: True or False; if TRUE: next line is executed; if FALSE: next line is skipped.

Parameter.Name: ReadOutTime
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns time of most recent physical IOPoint access.

Statement.Description: The SetLampSet statement allows to switch sources of light on or off and to arrange groups of lamps.
Statement.Name: SetLampSet

Parameter.Name: LampSet
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select a lamp set from the drop down list.

Statement.Description: The SetLightingSet statement allows activating sets of lamps, which have been predefined within the Lamp Definition panel.
Statement.Name: SetLightingSet

Parameter.Name: Lighting set
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select a lamp set from the drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Set
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select a space mouse parameter set from drop down list. The space mouse parameter set has to be defined before within the SpaceMouse configuration panel.

Statement.Description: This function reads the actual value from a selected power meter.
Statement.Name: GetPower

Parameter.Name: PowerMeter
Parameter.Description: Select a power meter from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Power
Parameter.Description: Retrieves actual value of selected power meter.

Statement.Description: Get the current current value from the selected LDC device.

Parameter.Name: Device name

Parameter.Name: Current (mA)
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns the current current value of the device [mA].

Statement.Description: Get the current voltage value from the selected LDC device.

Parameter.Name: Device name

Parameter.Name: Device name
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select device from list.

Parameter.Name: CurrentSetPoint (mA)
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns the setpoint for the current [mA] .

Statement.Description: Gets the status of the laser for the specified LDC device.

Parameter.Name: Device name

Parameter.Name: CurrentLimit (mA)
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Set the maximum current limit [mA].

Statement.Description: Set the status of the laser for the specified LDC device.

Parameter.Name: Device name
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select device from list.

Parameter.Name: LaserStatus
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Set the status of the laser. [True = ON, False = OFF]

Statement.Description: LDCGet_Error queries the profile mainframe error queue. The error number is returned. See the PRO 8000 / PRO 800 manual for a table of the error codes and their meaning.
Statement.Name: LDCGet_Error

Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select device from list.

Parameter.Name: Error
Parameter.Description: Result: Error number; error codes and their meaning are described in the hardware manual.

Statement.Description: This function activates the autorange function. In set mode this statement enables or disables the autorange function of the TIA hardware driver. In get mode the actual state of the autoranging function is returned.
Statement.Name: TIAAutorange

Parameter.Name: Meter
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select a power meter from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Function
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Switch between get and set: Get: actual state of autorange is returned. Set: disables autorange function of FEMTO hardware driver.

Parameter.Name: AutorangeIn
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Set an autorange value.

Parameter.Name: AutorangeOut
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns an autorange value.

Statement.Description: Sets or reads back the Reset AutoNull-Function state of the TIA meter selected. This function is disabled.
Statement.Name: TIACoupling

Parameter.Name: Meter
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select a power meter from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Function
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Switch between get and set: Get: read back FEMTOR amplifier. Set: set FEMTO amplifier.

Parameter.Name: CouplingIn
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select current mode: AC: select alternating current. DC: select direct current.

Parameter.Name: CouplingOut
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns coupling state.

Statement.Description: This statement activates a before within the TIA panel defined TIA parameter set.
Statement.Name: TIALoadSet

Parameter.Name: Meter
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select a power meter from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Set
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select a Femto parameter set from drop down list.

Statement.Description: Sets or reads back the actual offset of the TIA meter selected. This function is disabled.
Statement.Name: TIAOffset

Parameter.Name: Meter
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select a power meter from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Function
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Switch between get and set: Get: actual mode of FEMTO hardware driver is used. Set: set FEMTO mode.

Parameter.Name: OffsetIn
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Set offset value in a range between+-10V.

Parameter.Name: OffsetOut
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns offset value.

Statement.Description: This statement sets or reads back the actual polarity of the selected TIA meter. The values are Non-Inverting and Inverting.
Statement.Name: TIAPolarity

Parameter.Name: Meter
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select a power meter from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Function
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select a power meter from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: ModeIn
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Switch between High Speed and Low Noise Mode.

Parameter.Name: ModeOut
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Returns Femto mode.

Statement.Description: Using this statement the measurement range of the selected TIA amplifier can be set or read back. Available ranges are 0-5, where 5 is the least sensitive and 0 is the most sensitive range.
Statement.Name: TIARange

Parameter.Name: Meter
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select a power meter from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Function
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Switch between get and set: Get: actual mode of FEMTO hardware driver is used. Set: set FEMTO mode.

Parameter.Name: GainIn
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter range between 0 and 6; 0 is least sensitive, 6 most sensitive ranges.

Parameter.Name: GainOut
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns range value.

Statement.Description: Using this statement the wavelength of the selected TIA amplifier can be set or read back.
Statement.Name: TIAWavelength

Parameter.Name: Meter
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select a power meter from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Function
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Switch between get and set: Get: actual mode of FEMTO hardware driver is used. Set: set FEMTO mode.

Parameter.Name: WavelengthIn
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter wavelength.

Parameter.Name: WavelengthOut
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Returns wave length.

Statement.Description: Writes the calibration information to a file.
Statement.Name: TIAWriteCalibrationFile

Parameter.Name: Calibration File
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select a power meter from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Wavelength [nm]
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter a wavelength in nm.

Parameter.Name: Calibration Value [A/W]
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter a calibration value in A/W.

Statement.Description: This statement enables the general behaviour to stop the axis as soon as an external fault for the specified Axis is reported. It sets the stage to be decelerated but not disabled if an External Fault is active. If this behaviour is active, Error dialogs related to the external fault for this specific axis are suppressed.
Statement.Name: A3200_Activate_MoveUntilValue

Parameter.Name: Stage name
Parameter.Description: Name of the Axis

Parameter.Name: Enable
Parameter.Description: Control to enable or disables the behaviour.

Statement.Description: This statement add IOPoint(A3200-4_StageSignal) for record, which can then be started with the "STMT_A3200-4_StartRecord". The Address of IOPoint is: ASS:#, where #-signal number.(see AxisSignalTable.txt)
Statement.Name: A3200_AddAxisIOPointlToRecord

Parameter.Name: Stage
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select stage from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: IOPoint
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select IOPoint from drop down list.

Statement.Description: Performs a circular move on the two indicated axes.
Statement.Name: A3200_CircularMotion

Parameter.Name: Axis 1
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select stage from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Axis 2
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select stage from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Endpoint rel/abs
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select if the stage should run to an absolute position or to a position relative to the current position. Relative=stage moves to a position relative to the current position; Absolute=stage moves to an absolute position.

Parameter.Name: Axis1 Endpoint
Parameter.Description: The final position in user units on axis 1 at which to end the move.

Parameter.Name: Axis2 Endpoint
Parameter.Description: The final position in user units on axis 2 at which to end the move.

Parameter.Name: Center rel/abs
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select if the center of the circle is given in relative coordinates to the current position or as absolute coordinates.

Parameter.Name: Axis1 Center
Parameter.Description: The coordinate in user units on axis 1 of the center of the circle.

Parameter.Name: Axis2 Center
Parameter.Description: The coordinate in user units on axis 2 of the center of the circle.

Parameter.Name: Speed
Parameter.Description: The maximum linear speed in user units per second at which to travel along the circle.

Parameter.Name: Direction
Parameter.Description: Direction of movement (CW/CCW).

Parameter.Name: Sync
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select if the move process is executed synchronous to other sequences steps or not: Sync= no other sequence steps are executed until move has finished; No sync= move process is executed while other sequence steps are running.

Statement.Description: This statement configures the analog threshold that is being watched of the stage's analog channel. The stage will move until the threshold value is met and stop at that point. Please note : The general behaviour has to be activated using the Statement STMT_A3200_Activate_MoveUntilValue beforehand.
Statement.Name: A3200_Config_MoveUntilValue_Analog

Parameter.Name: Stage name
Parameter.Description: Name of the Axis

Parameter.Name: AnalogChannel
Parameter.Description: Number of the analog input for the defined axis

Parameter.Name: ThresholdValue
Parameter.Description: Upper threshold value for the external fault,

Parameter.Name: Enable
Parameter.Description: Control to enable or disables the behaviour.

Statement.Description: This statement configures the digital channel that is being watched of the stage's digital channels. The stage will move until the digital value at that channel is met and stop at that point. Please note : The general behaviour has to be activated using the Statement STMT_A3200_Activate_MoveUntilValue beforehand.
Statement.Name: A3200_Config_MoveUntilValue_Digital

Parameter.Name: Stage name
Parameter.Description: Name of the Axis

Parameter.Name: DigitalChannel
Parameter.Description: Number of the digital input for the defined axis

Parameter.Name: Enable
Parameter.Description: Control to enable or disables the behaviour.

Statement.Description: This statement reads a parameter for the selected axis.
Statement.Name: A3200_GetAxisParameterNumeric

Parameter.Name: Stage name
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select stage from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Parameter
Parameter.Description: Pick one of the listed parameter names.

Statement.Description: This statement reads a parameter for the selected axis.
Statement.Name: A3200_GetAxisParameterString

Parameter.Name: Stage name
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select stage from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Parameter
Parameter.Description: Pick one of the listed parameter names.

Statement.Description: This statement indicates the current state of the stage. This is used for example if an automated PGM profile is executed (see STMT_A3200-4_StartProgram). The corresponding states are:Unavailable = 0; Inactive = 1; Idle = 2; ProgramReady = 3; ProgramRunning =4; ProgramFeedheld = 5; ProgramPaused = 6; ProgramComplete = 7; Error = 8; Queue = 9; PLCReserved = 10.
Statement.Name: A3200_GetState

Parameter.Name: State
Parameter.Description: This output indicates the current state of the stage. The corresponding states are: Unavailable = 0; Inactive = 1; Idle = 2; ProgramReady = 3; ProgramRunning =4; ProgramFeedheld = 5; ProgramPaused = 6; ProgramComplete = 7; Error = 8; Queue = 9; PLCReserved = 10.

Statement.Description: This statement initializes a new recocd-set, which can then be started with the statement "A3200-4_StartRecord".
Statement.Name: A3200_InitRecord

Parameter.Name: Record path
Parameter.Description: Parameter: the filename definition for record data can be absolute, symbolic or file name, in the last case, the data is written to the SM-Data folder. (Example: TestData.csv; #SM_DATA#\TestData.csv; C:\Data\TestData.csv)

Parameter.Name: Rate
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Sampling rate in samples per channel per second .

Statement.Description: This statement loads a PGM file, that can then be started using the "STMT_A3200-4_StartProgram". The path definition has to be absolute.
Statement.Name: A3200_LoadProgram

Parameter.Name: Program path
Parameter.Description: Parameter: This defines the path and filename to the PGM file that should be loaded. The path definition has to be absolute.

Statement.Description: This statement defines an parameter for the selected axis.
Statement.Name: A3200_SetAxisParameterNumeric

Parameter.Name: Stage name
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select stage from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Parameter
Parameter.Description: Pick one of the listed parameter names.

Parameter.Name: Value
Parameter.Description: Parameter: define parameter value.

Statement.Description: This statement defines an parameter for the selected axis.
Statement.Name: A3200_SetAxisParameterString

Parameter.Name: Stage name
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select stage from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Parameter
Parameter.Description: Pick one of the listed parameter names.

Parameter.Name: Value
Parameter.Description: Parameter: define parameter value

Statement.Description: This statements starts a previously loaded program (see STMT_A3200-4_LoadProgram) and either can wait till this is finished (Parameter: Wait Until Completer = True) or not.
Statement.Name: A3200_StartProgram

Parameter.Name: Wait Until Completed
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Signals whether the Statement should wait on completion of the executed Program.

Statement.Description: This statements starts record a previously configured set.
Statement.Name: A3200_StartRecord

Statement.Description: This statement stopps a prevously started PGM profile.
Statement.Name: A3200_StopProgram

Statement.Description: This statement stops a previously started record set.
Statement.Name: A3200_StopRecord

Statement.Description:
Locks the front panel of the uv lamp. Returns a message.
06.03.2014, CK, V1.0

Parameter.Name: Device
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select device from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Message
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns the ASCII string read from the selected device.

Statement.Description:
Query a command from the uv lamp. Calculates a crc8, adds it to the command, send the command, reads out the answer, strips the crc8 and returns the status.

Statement.Description: Performs a fast 2D-, 3D-, or 4D-Align controlled by the motion board. The used stages as well as the power measurement input must be connected to the same controller board. The definition of the parameter sets can be performed in the "FastAlign" Panel. The final positions after for all used postioning devices is returned as well as the maximum measured intensity.
Statement.Name: AdvAlign_FastScan

Statement.Description: Performs a fast 2D-, 3D-, or 4D-Align controlled by the motion board. The used stages as well as the power measurement input must be connected to the same controller board. The definition of the parameter sets can be performed in the "FastAlign" Panel. The final positions after for all used postioning devices is returned as well as the maximum measured intensity.
Statement.Name: AdvAlign_GeoCenter2D

Statement.Description: Performs a fast 2D-, 3D-, or 4D-Align controlled by the motion board. The used stages as well as the power measurement input must be connected to the same controller board. The definition of the parameter sets can be performed in the "FastAlign" Panel. The final positions after for all used postioning devices is returned as well as the maximum measured intensity.
Statement.Name: AdvAlign_Hillclimb

Statement.Description: Performs a fast 2D-, 3D-, or 4D-Align controlled by the motion board. The used stages as well as the power measurement input must be connected to the same controller board. The definition of the parameter sets can be performed in the "FastAlign" Panel. The final positions after for all used postioning devices is returned as well as the maximum measured intensity.
Statement.Name: AdvAlign_SpiralScan

Parameter.Name: Threshold optional
Parameter.Description: Threshold value for SpiralRough operation only. If a Threshold value <> 0 is entered this value overwrittes the one given in the selected parameter set.

Parameter.Name: Threshld Found
Parameter.Description: Inidicates success in finding threshold value (relevant for SpiralRough only)

Statement.Description: This statement can be used for abort the Advance Alignment Scan.
Statement.Name: AbortScan

Statement.Description: This function performs a Blindsearch. If a set up is chosen the parameters saved within the Blindsearch panel for this set up are used and no other parameters need to be configured. Is **** chosen as set up, the parameters entered in the Sequence Editor are activated. The definition of the parameters corresponds to the parameters of the Blindsearch panel. The statement returns 0 for no light found during Blindsearch and 1 for light found during Blindsearch.
Statement.Name: Blindsearch

Parameter.Name: Setup
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Activate parameters entered in the sequence by ****. If a Parameter Set is selected from the drop down list, the chosen parameters set is activated.

Parameter.Name: Meter
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select power meter from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Scan stage
Parameter.Description: "Parameter: Select X-axis for alignment from list; horizontal / orthogonal to optical axis."

Parameter.Name: Step stage
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select Y-axis for alignment from list, vertical / orthogonal to optical axis.

Parameter.Name: Digital Line
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select digital I/O line from list the power meter trigger signal is connected to.

Parameter.Name: Scan range (µm)
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Define length of scan line in µm.

Parameter.Name: Scan speed (µm)
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Define maximum speed of x stage while moving trough a single line in µm/sec.

Parameter.Name: Step width (µm)
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Defines distance between single scan lines in µm.

Parameter.Name: Step range (µm)
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Defines search area in Y direction in µm.

Statement.Description: This function performs a Hillclimb. If a set up is chosen the parameters saved within the Hillclimb Panel for this set up are used and no other parameters need to be configured. Is **** chosen as set up, the parameters entered in the Sequence Editor are activated. The definition of the parameters corresponds to the parameters of the Hillclimb panel. While the Hillclimb is running a plot pops up displaying the status of the Hillclimb.
Statement.Name: Hillclimb

Parameter.Name: Setup
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Activate parameters entered in the sequence by ****. If a Parameter Set is selected from the drop down list, the chosen parameters set is activated.

Parameter.Name: Meter
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select power meter from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: X stage
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select X-axis from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Y stage
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select Y-axis from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Z stage
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select Z-axis from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Search Step X (µm)
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Define search step in X direction in µm.

Parameter.Name: Search Step Y (µm)
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Define search step in Y direction in µm.

Parameter.Name: Search Step Z (µm)
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Define search step in Z direction in µm.

Parameter.Name: Factor X
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Define how the intensity profile in X direction measured during search step is weighed by Hillclimb algorithm.

Parameter.Name: Factor Y
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Define how the intensity profile in Y direction measured during search step is weighed by Hillclimb algorithm.

Parameter.Name: Factor Z
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Define how the intensity profile in Z direction measured during search step is weighed by Hillclimb algorithm.

Parameter.Name: InitialStepwidth(µm)
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter initial step width in µm. Hillclimb will be aborted, if intensity is higher than power stop level.

Parameter.Name: Stop at -%
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter percentage of maximum intensity. Hillclimb will be aborted if measurement intensity has decreased by given percentage of maximum intensity.

Parameter.Name: PowerStopLevel (µw]
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter power stop level. Hillclimb will be aborted, if intensity is higher than power stop level.

Statement.Description: This function performs a SpiralBlindsearch. If a set up is chosen the parameters saved within the Spiral Blindsearch panel for this set up are used and no other parameters need to be configured. Is **** chosen as set up, the parameters entered in the Sequence Editor are activated. The definition of the parameters corresponds to the parameters of the Spiral Blindsearch panel. The statement returns 0 for no light found during Spiral Blindsearch and 1 for light found during Spiral Blindsearch.
Statement.Name: SpiralBlindsearch

Parameter.Name: Setup
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Activate parameters entered in the sequence by ****. If a Parameter Set is selected from the drop down list, the chosen parameters set is activated.

Parameter.Name: Meter
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select power meter from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Scan stage
Parameter.Description: "Parameter: Select X-axis for alignment from list; horizontal / orthogonal to optical axis."

Parameter.Name: Step stage
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select Y-axis for alignment from list, vertical / orthogonal to optical axis.

Parameter.Name: Digital Line
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select digital I/O line from list the power meter trigger signal is connected to.

Parameter.Name: Scan range (µm)
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Define length of scan line in µm.

Parameter.Name: Scan speed (µm)
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Define maximum speed of x and y stage while moving trough a single line in µm/sec.

Parameter.Name: Step width (µm)
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Define distance between single scan lines in µm.

Parameter.Name: Trigger level (µW)
Parameter.Description: "Parameter: Define intensity at which SpiralBlindsearch stops; if trigger is reached during the line search operation was successful. Note: Trigger level must also be set in the power meter panel before starting SpiralBlindsearch."

Statement.Description: This function performs an XY Scan. If a set up is chosen the parameters saved within the XY Scan Panel for this set up are used and no other parameters need to be configured. Is **** chosen as set up, the parameters entered in the Sequence Editor are activated. The definition of the parameters corresponds to the parameters of the XY Scan panel. The function returns the maximum intensities found during the X Scan and the Y Scan.
Statement.Name: XY Scan

Parameter.Name: Setup
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Activate parameters entered in the sequence by ****. If a Parameter Set is selected from the drop down list, the chosen parameters set is activated.

Parameter.Name: XScan range (µm)
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Define length of Y scan line in µm.

Parameter.Name: XStep width (µm)
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Define distance between single scan lines in X direction in µm.

Parameter.Name: YScan range(µm)
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Define length of Y scan line in µm.

Parameter.Name: YStep width(µm)
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Defines distance between single scan lines in Y direction in µm.

Parameter.Name: Max X Pos
Parameter.Description: Result: X position of maximum found intensity.

Parameter.Name: Max Int X
Parameter.Description: Result: Maximum intensity found during x scan.

Parameter.Name: Max Y Pos
Parameter.Description: Result: Y position of maximum found intensity.

Parameter.Name: Max Int Y
Parameter.Description: Result: Maximum intensity found during y scan.

Statement.Description: This function performs a XYZ Scan. If a set up is chosen the parameters saved within the XYZ Scan panel for this set up are used and no other parameters need to be configured. Parameter.Name: X stage
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select X-axis for alignment from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Y stage
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select X-axis for alignment from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Z stage

Parameter.Name: XScan range (µm)
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Define length of X scan line in µm.

Parameter.Name: XStep width (µm)
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Define distance between single scan lines in X direction in µm.

Parameter.Name: YScan range (µm)
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Define length of Y scan line in µm.

Statement.Description: Extracts depending on the selection, either the row or the column from the 2D Array and outputs it to an new array.
Statement.Name: ArrayHandling_ExtractArray

Parameter.Name: ArraySourceName
Parameter.Description: Name of the source Array.

Parameter.Name: ArrayNewName
Parameter.Description: Name of the new Array.

Parameter.Name: ExtractType
Parameter.Description: Defines whether to extract a row or a column from the 2D array.

Parameter.Name: Index
Parameter.Description: Defines the index number of the row or column to extract from the array.

Parameter.Name: Found?
Parameter.Description: Defines whether the Source Array could be found.

Statement.Description: Determines either the Min, Max or mean value for the given Array and outputs the Index (for 1D Index-X) of the ArrayElement (only for Min,Max) and the Value.
Statement.Name: ArrayHandling_MathOperation

Parameter.Name: ArrayName
Parameter.Description: Name of the source Array.

Parameter.Name: Operation
Parameter.Description: This is the operation that should be executed. It can be one of the following : Min,Max,Mean

Parameter.Name: Index-X
Parameter.Description: The Index position of the Min or Max element. In case of Mean this value is NaN. In case of an 2D Array this is the X coordinate of the array element.

Parameter.Name: Index-Y
Parameter.Description: This is only used for 2D Arrays and outputs the Y Index for the Min or Max location. Otherwise this element is NaN.

Parameter.Name: Value
Parameter.Description: This is the value for the Min, Max or Mean of the Array.

Parameter.Name: Found?
Parameter.Description: Defines whether the Source Array could be found

Statement.Description: All text in the error display is cleared.
Statement.Name: DisplayClearError

Statement.Description: Displays a dialogue with one OK button. The text on the buttons may be set by sequence parameters. Additionally, the position of this dialogue on the screen may be specified as TopPos and LeftPos in screen coordinates for the upper left corner of the dialogue box. Note: If possible please always use this dialogue function, because less system resources are required.
Statement.Name: DisplayExtdDialog_Scaled

Parameter.Name: Dialog text
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter the dialog text to be displayed.

Parameter.Name: LeftPos
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Choose screen coordinates for the left corner of the dialog box.

Parameter.Name: TopPos
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Choose screen coordinates for the upper corner of the dialog box.

Parameter.Name: Window Title
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter the window title.

Parameter.Name: Button text
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter the text for the first button (OK).

Parameter.Name: Scaling Factor
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter the display scaling factor (1 .. 5) for the dialog.

Statement.Description: Displays a dialogue with 2 buttons, one for OK (the next sequence statement will be executed), and one for SKIP (the next statement will be skipped). The text on the buttons may be set by sequence parameters. Additionally, the position of this dialogue on the screen may be specified as TopPos and LeftPos in screen coordinates for the upper left corner of the dialogue box. Note: If possible please always use this dialogue function, because less system resources are required.
Statement.Name: DisplayExtdSelectionDialog_Scaled

Parameter.Name: Dialog text
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter the dialog text to be displayed.

Parameter.Name: LeftPos
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Choose screen coordinates for the left corner of the dialog box.

Parameter.Name: TopPos
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Choose screen coordinates for the upper corner of the dialog box.

Parameter.Name: Window Title
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter the window title.

Parameter.Name: Button 1 (OK) text
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter the text for the first button (OK).

Parameter.Name: Button 2 (Skip) text
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter the text for the second button (SKIP).

Parameter.Name: Scaling Factor
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter the display scaling factor (1 .. 5) for the dialog.

Statement.Description: Displays a simple dialog, with Title, Dialog text and Button text. The dialog can be positioned using TopPos and LeftPos in screen coordinates for the upper left corner of the dialog box. Note: If possible please always use this dialog function, because less system resources are required.
Statement.Name: DropDownDialog

Parameter.Name: Dialog text
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter the dialog text to be displayed.

Parameter.Name: TopPos
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Choose screen coordinates for the upper corner of the dialog box.

Parameter.Name: LeftPos
Parameter.Description: Parameter : Choose screen coordinates for the left corner of the dialog box.

Parameter.Name: Title
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter the dialog title.

Parameter.Name: Button text
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter the button text.

Statement.Description: Clears the values of the defined MiniReport Panel.

Statement.Description: Closes the MiniReport Panel using the Titel from the MiniReportOpen Statement

Statement.Description: Opens the MiniReport Panel, assigns the Title to the panel.

Parameter.Name: Titel
Parameter.Description: Name of the MiniReport Panel.

Statement.Description: Adds a new entry to the MiniReport Panel.
Statement.Name: MiniReportWriteNumber

Parameter.Name: Titel
Parameter.Description: Name of the MiniReport Panel.

Parameter.Name: Field
Parameter.Description: Number of the field that should be edited. The fields are numbered from top to bottom and continued in the table to the right. Each table is 10 fields long. (Example: Col 1 Row 1 -> Field 2, Col 2 Row 1 -> Field 11)

Parameter.Name: Description
Parameter.Description: The descriptive text that should be displayed in the first MiniReport column.

Parameter.Name: Number
Parameter.Description: The numeric value that should be displayed in the second MiniReport column.

Statement.Description: Adds a new entry to the MiniReport Panel.
Statement.Name: MiniReportWriteString

Parameter.Name: Field
Parameter.Description: Number of the field that should be edited. The fields are numbered from top to bottom and continued in the table to the right. Each table is 10 fields long. (Example: Col 1 Row 1 -> Field 2, Col 2 Row 1 -> Field 11)

Parameter.Name: Description
Parameter.Description: The descriptive text that should be displayed in the first MiniReport column.

Parameter.Name: String
Parameter.Description: The string value that should be displayed in the second MiniReport column.

Statement.Description: This statement takes as the input the VI Reference name, which corresponds in most of the times in case of a panel to the panel Vi Name, a format and a path where the image should be saved. As a result it is returned whether a screenshot could be successfully generated or not.
Statement.Name: Panel_Screenshot

Parameter.Name: VIReference
Parameter.Description: This is the VI Reference name, which corresponds in most of the cases to the panel Vi Name.

Parameter.Name: Format
Parameter.Description: This is the type of file that should be saved to the filelocation defined. It can be chosen between: BMP,JPG and PNG.

Parameter.Name: Filepath
Parameter.Description: This is the filepath to the Screenshot file that should be written. The path can be defined with TestMaster specific naming (e.g. "#SM_DATA\Screenshot.png)

Parameter.Name: Success
Parameter.Description: This indicates whether the screenshot could be generated successfully

Statement.Description: Determines the focus position of the camera by moving if for a distance "ScanLenght". The "Method" for determining the focus can be selected.
Statement.Name: IMAQ_AutoFocus

Parameter.Name: Setup
Parameter.Description: Select a valid set from the list. Using set "****" you can define the parameters yourself using the following inputs.

Parameter.Name: Camera
Parameter.Description: Camera device used for the autofocus function.

Parameter.Name: Axis
Parameter.Description: Stage device used for the autofocus function.

Parameter.Name: Mode
Parameter.Description: Method used for autofocus.

Parameter.Name: Method
Parameter.Description: Filter applied to image before the focus quality is evaluated.

Parameter.Name: Filter
Parameter.Description: Defines Filters that can be applied to an image before processing it.

Parameter.Name: Steps
Parameter.Description: Scan steps used during autofocus.

Parameter.Name: Left
Parameter.Description: Upper left x-position of the ROI.

Parameter.Name: Top
Parameter.Description: Top y-position of the ROI.

Parameter.Name: Right
Parameter.Description: Lower right x-position of the ROI.

Parameter.Name: Bottom
Parameter.Description: Bottom y-position of the ROI.

Parameter.Name: Show pic?
Parameter.Description: Selection (Hide, Show) whether to open a camera picture and show the ROI and the autofocus.

Parameter.Name: UseRaw
Parameter.Description: Sets whether to use the raw or the fittet data.

Parameter.Name: Focus
Parameter.Description: Returns whether a focus was found. Also False in case of an error.

Parameter.Name: Max
Parameter.Description: Returns whether a maximum was found.

Parameter.Description: This is the reference from the panel that is created when the panel is opened.

Parameter.Name: OverlayGroups
Parameter.Description: Specifies the overlay groups (comma seperated string) which are added to the image. (e.g. Overlay1,Overlay2)

Statement.Description: This statements enabled the left mouse button in the ShowPicture panels to reposition the current cursor position. The user can define to either use the "Enter" or "Space" button to confirm his input.
Statement.Name: IMAQ_ShowPicture_SetCursor

Parameter.Name: PanelReference
Parameter.Description: This is the Panel reference generated from the Statement "IMAQ_ShowPicture". It references the panel that should be used by this statement.

Parameter.Name: DisplayText
Parameter.Description: This is the text that is displayed on the screen. If the Text is left blank, the Standard Text

Parameter.Name: Position
Parameter.Description: This enum defines at which position the DisplayText will be displayed on the panel.s

Parameter.Name: ButtonName
Parameter.Description: This is the name of the button that should be used to confirm the current position.

Parameter.Name: Success
Parameter.Description: This return value indicates whether the position was successfully changed with the panel. If the variable is false, either the user aborted the process (by pressing the Esc button) or he did not change the current position.

Parameter.Name: InputValue
Parameter.Description: The input value.

Parameter.Name: AbsoluteValue
Parameter.Description: The absolute ReturnValue.

Statement.Description: Computes the integer quotient and the remainder of the inputs.
Statement.Name: Math_Modulo

Parameter.Name: x
Parameter.Description: The input X value.

Parameter.Name: y
Parameter.Description: The input Y value.

Parameter.Name: Remainder
Parameter.Description: The remainder computed by x-y*floor(x/y).

Parameter.Name: Quotient
Parameter.Description: The quotient computed by floor(x/y).

Statement.Description: Round the input value by the selected method [floor,ceil,nearest].
Statement.Name: Math_Round

Parameter.Name: InputValue
Parameter.Description: The input value that should be rounded.

Parameter.Name: Method
Parameter.Description: Select the function to round by [floor,ceil,nearest].

Parameter.Name: ReturnValue
Parameter.Description: The rounded return value.

Statement.Description: Returns to a given Intensity level scanning the given axis in step size steps to a maximum distance Max abs. dist. in positive or negative Scan direction and searching for a threshold value starting at a higher value or lower value then the threshold. If the treshold is reached is returned in I found.
Statement.Name: MetrologyAdvancedMoveToIntensity

Parameter.Name: Axis
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select stage from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Meter
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select meter from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Step Size (µm)
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter step size in µm.

Parameter.Name: Max abs. dist. (µm)
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Maximum distance to be scanned.

Parameter.Name: Intensity level
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Intensity level to be reached.

Parameter.Name: Scan direction (True= Positive)
Parameter.Description: "Parameter: Select scan direction: True=positive scan direction; False=negative scan direction."

Parameter.Name: F=coming from max/ T=comming from min
I found
Parameter.Description: "Result: Returns if Intensity level has been found; 0=Level not found;1=Level found."

Statement.Description: This statement performs an Area Scan. The parameters correspond to the identical parameters in the AreaScan user panel. If the Scan is aborted by the user the returned results are evaluated on the basis of the actual acquired data and the Stages return to the start position.
Statement.Name: MetrologyAreaScan

Parameter.Name: Setup
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Activate parameters entered in the sequence by ****. If a Parameter Set is selected from the drop down list, the chosen parameters set is activated.

Parameter.Name: Timeout
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Defines the maximal timeout duration when the ViewAreaScan Panel (if ShowScan = True) will be closed.

Parameter.Name: ShowScan?
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select, if the scan is shown as a graph in the display area of the Line Scan panel: True=Scan is displayed; False=Scan is not displayed.

Parameter.Name: Meter
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select a meter from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: X Stage
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select x stage from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Y Stage
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select y stage from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Scan range X (µm)
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Define scan length in x direction in µm.

Parameter.Name: Step width X (µm)
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Define step width in x direction in µm.

Parameter.Name: Scan range Y (µm)
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Define scan length in y direction in µm.

Parameter.Name: Step width Y (µm)
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Define step width in y direction in µm.

Parameter.Name: Velocity
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Define velocity for scan

Parameter.Name: StartPos
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Define strt position for scan

Parameter.Name: StopTolerance
Parameter.Description: Parameters: Defines tolerance of MaxValue to end measurement

Parameter.Name: Return to peak?
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Choose if axes should remove to peak or not: true= return to peak; false=return to the start position.

Parameter.Name: Filename
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Path to the CSV file where the AreaScan data should be saved to. TM Path definitions are allowed. If the path is left empty (empty string), the data won't be saved.

Parameter.Name: Max Int
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns maximum intensity in %.

Parameter.Name: Max Pos X
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns position of maximum intensity on x-axis.

Parameter.Name: Max Pos Y
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns position of maximum intensity on y-axis.

Parameter.Name: aborted
Parameter.Description: indicates if the Scan was aborted by user interaction

Statement.Description: This statement can be used to display the data acquired by an Area Scan within an intensity graph. It is possible to control the behavior of the Area Scan Display panel programmatically in several ways which is not possible when using the ShowScan? option of the AreaScan statement itself. It is very important to create the AreaScanDisplay before performing the AreaScan.
Statement.Name: MetrologyAreaScanDisplay

Parameter.Name: Operation
Parameter.Description: "Parameter: Function: Create=Opens a communication reference to the AreaScan function. It is important to call the create operation before starting an AreaScan: Dispose=Closes the reference to the AreaScan function. If the AreaScan display panel is still open, the panel will be closed; Show=Shows the AreaScan Display panel; Hide=Hides the AreaScan Display panel; Show Exit Button=Shows the exit button of the AreaScan Display so the operator can close the display interactively; Hide Exit Button=Hides the exit button of the AreaScan Display so the operator can not close the display; Clear Graph=Clears the graph data within the AreaScan display."
Parameter.EnumStrings: Clear Graph,Create,Dispose,Hide,Hide Exit Button,Show,Show Exit Button

Statement.Description: "This statement performs a DoubleLineScan with two power meters and returns the resulting Intensity and Position for both power meters. The returned values are correlated with the value given in the Return to parameter. The parameters correspond to the parameters in the Line Scan panel and the LineScan sets are used with the only difference that the Meter 2 has to be set because this meter is not foreseen in the LineScan panel and there is no special DoubleLineScan panel. Is **** chosen as setup, the parameters entered in the Sequence Editor are activated; otherwise the chosen parameter set is activated but you have to set Meter 2. If the Scan is aborted by the user the returned results are evaluated on the basis of the actual acquired data and the Stages return to the start position "
Statement.Name: MetrologyDoubleLineScan

Parameter.Name: Setup
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Activate parameters entered in the sequence by ****. If a Parameter Set is selected from the drop down list, the chosen parameters set is activated. At all the Meter 2 has to be chosen.

Parameter.Name: ShowScan?
Parameter.Description: "Parameter: Select, if the scan is shown as a graph in the display area of the Line Scan panel: True=Scan is displayed; False=Scan is not displayed."

Parameter.Name: Timeout(ms)
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter timeout for ShowScan? in ms.

Parameter.Name: Meter
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select a meter from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Stage
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select a stage from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Scan range (µm)
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Define scan length in µm.

Parameter.Name: Step width (µm)
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Define step width in µm.

Parameter.Name: Return to
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Return to starting position, min or max.

Parameter.Name: Initial move?
Parameter.Description: "Parameter: Switch initial move on or off: true = on; false = off."
Parameter: % value of maximum at which to calculate the FWHM.

Parameter.Name: Poly Fit order
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter order of polynomial fit.

Parameter.Name: Meter 2
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select a second meter from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Fit Threshold (0-100)
Parameter.Description: The threshold value defines the values to be used for the fit.The This means minmum is 0% and 100 % max. threshold value is defined as Minum + threshold (%) * (Min-Max) The complete curve will be checked for the first value from the left and the first value from the right side exceeding the threshold value.

Parameter.Name: Save?
Parameter.Description: "Parameter: Decide to save the resulting; True=save on; False= save off."

Parameter.Name: FileName
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Result is saved in the data folder of the current process with the given name.

Parameter.Name: FWHM
Parameter.Description: Result: Width of scanned curve of meter at given % of maximum.

Parameter.Name: Max Pos
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns position of maximum, minimum, start intensity on Stage depending on the Return to position.

Parameter.Name: Max Int
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns maximum, minimum, start intensity.

Parameter.Name: Max Pos 2
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns second position of maximum, minimum, start intensity on Stage, depending on the Return to position.

Parameter.Name: Max Int 2
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns second maximum, minimum, start intensity.

Parameter.Name: FWHM 2
Parameter.Description: Result: Width of scanned curve of meter 2 at given % of maximum.

Parameter.Name: aborted
Parameter.Description: indicates if the Scan was aborted by user interaction

Statement.Description: This statement finds the overlapping area for 4 LineScans defined by their LineScan export files (see MetrologyLineScan). It therefore used a threshold and the FWHM method to determine the area that should be analysed for each LineScan and then combines them to find the region that is common to all of those graphs. The distance between the minimal ("[Lower]") and maximal ("[Upper]") edge theirby has to be at least [MinDistance] long to be accepted as a valid output. Otherwise the [Found] output is False and all return values are set to 0.
Statement.Name: MetrologyFindCommonPlateau

Parameter.Name: FormatStr
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Defines the formatting string for extracting LineScan data from the LineScan files. (Default: "%.3f" using System default separator)

Parameter.Name: File01
Parameter.Description: Parameter: File location to the exported LineScan data extracted with the Statement "MetrologyLineScan". (e.g. "#SM_DATA#\\LineScan1.txt")

Parameter.Name: File02
Parameter.Description: Parameter: File location to the exported LineScan data extracted with the Statement "MetrologyLineScan". (e.g. "#SM_DATA#\\LineScan1.txt")

Parameter.Name: File03
Parameter.Description: Parameter: File location to the exported LineScan data extracted with the Statement "MetrologyLineScan". (e.g. "#SM_DATA#\\LineScan1.txt")

Parameter.Name: File04
Parameter.Description: Parameter: File location to the exported LineScan data extracted with the Statement "MetrologyLineScan". (e.g. "#SM_DATA#\\LineScan1.txt")

Parameter.Name: Threshold
Parameter.Description: Parameter: The threshold parameter ("%") used for the FWHM evaluation of each LineScan.

Parameter.Name: MinDistance
Parameter.Description: Parameter: The minimal distance that has to be met between the minimal "[Lower]" and the maximal "[Upper]" found edges to accept the result as a result and signal the "[Found]" output as positive.

Parameter.Name: ShowPanel
Parameter.Description: Parameter: This switch, switches the visualization via the panel on (True) or off (False). If disabled the calculation is done based on the parameters automatically and the best result is returned.

Parameter.Name: Lower
Parameter.Description: Result: This output returns the lower edge location on the X axis that has been found. If the minimal distance ("[MinDistance]") is not met or there is no overlapping area found for all 4 files this output is set to 0.

Parameter.Name: Upper
Parameter.Description: Result: This output returns the upper edge location on the X axis that has been found. If the minimal distance ("[MinDistance]") is not met or there is no overlapping area found for all 4 files this output is set to 0.

Parameter.Name: Optimal
Parameter.Description: Result: This output returns the middle or if altered with the panel the altered location on the X axis that has been found between the minumum ("[Lower]") and maximum ("[Upper]") edges. If the minimal distance ("[MinDistance]") is not met or there is no overlapping area found for all 4 files this output is set to 0.

Parameter.Name: Found
Parameter.Description: Result: This output signals whether there has been found an overlapping area of all LineScans that has a minimal distance on the X axis defined by "[MinDistance]".

Statement.Description: This function can only be used after a LineScan, because the linescan data is necessary for the fit and movement. The scan direction gives the searching direction for the threshold. The fit is performed in the given range of lower/upper fit limit and it is important that no value outside the search range exceeds the treshold level you are looking for. If the threshold can be found in the search range of the fit the stage moves to the according position. If the threshold cannot be found within the search range you could use the interpolated position (up to second order) and then it is possible to move to the calculated position using the MoveStage command.
Statement.Name: MetrologyFitLineScanAndMoveToIntensity

Parameter.Name: Stage
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select stage from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: polynomial order
Parameter.Description: The polynomial order must be greater than or equal to zero: 0 and is less than or equal to m, which is less than n - 1, where n is the number of sample points, and m is the polynomial order. If polynomial order is less than zero, the VI sets Polynomial Fit Coefficients to an empty array and returns an error The default is 2.

Parameter.Name: Threshold y
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Threshold value the stage should be moved to.

Parameter.Name: Velocity user per sec
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Velocity with which stage will move to Threshold y.

Parameter.Name: Lower Fit Limit um
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Lower limit of stage position for search of Threshold y.

Parameter.Name: Upper Fit Limit um
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Upper limit of stage position for search of Threshold y.

Parameter.Name: Return to Treshold?
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Decide if you move automatically to the Threshold y. True: The Stage is moving to the threshold if the Threshold y is in range Lower FitLimit µm < Stage position < Upper Fit Limit µm. False: The Stage will not be moved in this Statement

Parameter.Name: Position um
Parameter.Description: Result: Positon of Threshold y after interpolation with polynomial order. NaN if out of Range.

Parameter.Name: In Range?
Parameter.Description: "Result: Returns if the found threshold is in the range Lower FitLimit µm < Stage position < Upper Fit Limit µm.True=It is in range; False=It is out of range."

Parameter.Name: Interpolated Position1
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns the fitted position of the threshold correlated with the calculated fit coefficients to the curve in the given range.

Parameter.Name: Interpolated Position2
Parameter.Description: Result: For second order fit two fit values are possible.

Statement.Description: " This statement performs a Line Scan. The parameters correspond to the same parameters in the Line Scan panel. Is **** chosen as setup, the parameters entered in the Sequence Editor are activated; otherwise the chosen parameter set is activated."
Statement.Name: MetrologyLineScan

Parameter.Name: Setup
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Activate parameters entered in the sequence by ****. If a Parameter Set is selected from the drop down list, the chosen parameters set is activated.

Parameter.Name: ShowScan?

Parameter.Name: Timeout(ms)
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter timeout for ShowScan? in ms.
Parameter: % value of maximum at which to calculate the FWHM.

Parameter.Name: Meter

Parameter.Name: Stage

Parameter.Description: Parameter: Define step width in µm.

Parameter.Name: Scan range (µm)
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Define scan length in µm.

Parameter.Name: Speed
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Define speed in µm/s.(Nan=Automatic:Positioning speed and Return speed - Scan_range x1000; Measurement speed- Scan_range x100)

Parameter.Name: Return to
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Return to starting position, min or max.

Parameter.Name: Initial move?
Parameter.Description: "Parameter: Switch initial move on or off: true = on; false = off."

Parameter.Name: GaussFit
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter order of polynomial fit.

Parameter.Name: Save?
Parameter.Description: "Parameter: Decide to save the resulting; True=save on; False= save off."

Parameter.Name: FileName
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Result is saved in the data folder of the current process with the given name.

Parameter.Name: FWHM
Parameter.Description: Result: Width of scanned curve of Meter at given % of maximum.

Parameter.Name: Max Pos
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns position of maximum, minimum, start intensity on Stage, depending on the Return to position.

Parameter.Name: Max Int
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns maximum, minimum, start intensity.

Statement.Description: This statement is similar to the Move to intensity level function of the Line Scan panel. The Stage will be moved in positive scan direction until a certain Intensity level or the Max abs . dist. (µm) is reached . The parameters correspond to the same parameters in the Line Scan panel. If the given statement found the given intensity level, the intensity found returns 1 otherwise it returns 0. The scan direction is always positive.
Statement.Name: MetrologyMoveToIntensity

Parameter.Name: Axis
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select stage from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Meter
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select meter from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Step Size (µm)
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter step size in µm.

Parameter.Name: Max abs. dist. (µm)
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Maximal search range.

Parameter.Name: Intensity level
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Defines the Intensity level which has to be found.

Parameter.Name: I found
Parameter.Description: "Result: Returns if Intensity level has been found; 0=Level not found;1=Level found."

Statement.Description: This statement records the data aquired during a Line Scan. The statement starts the line scan and then records the data of up to 4 different Meters defined ([Meter1], [Meter2], [Meter3], [Meter4]). The Stage Parameters([Stage],[Step width (µm)],[Scan range (µm)],[Speed],[Initial move?],[ScanDirection]) are identical to the ones used with the normal LineScan Statement. The user has to define 4 files that are recorded ([File01],[File02],[File03],[File04]).
Statement.Name: MetrologyRecordLineScans

Parameter.Name: Meter1

Parameter.Name: Meter2
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select a meter from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Meter3
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select a meter from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Meter4
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select a meter from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Stage
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select a stage from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Step width (µm)
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Define step width in µm.

Parameter.Name: Scan range (µm)
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Define scan length in µm.

Parameter.Name: Speed
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Define speed in µm/s.(Nan=Automatic:Positioning speed and Return speed - Scan_range x1000; Measurement speed- Scan_range x100)

Parameter.Name: Initial move?
Parameter.Description: "Parameter: Switch initial move on or off: true = on; false = off."

Statement.Description: This statement saves the data aquired with a LineScan previously with their absolute position values.
Statement.Name: MetrologySaveAbsoluteLineScanData

Parameter.Name: File
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Result is saved in the data folder of the current process with the given name.

Statement.Description: This statement can be used to display the data acquired by an optical metrology scan within an corresponding graph. It is possible to control the behavior of the Scan Display panel programmatically in several ways. It is required to create the AreaScanDisplay before performing the Scan.
Statement.Name: MetrologyScanDisplay

Parameter.Name: Operation
Parameter.Description: "Parameter: Function: Create=Opens a communication reference to the Scan function. It is required to call the create operation before starting an Scan: Dispose=Closes the reference to the Scan function. If the Scan display panel is still open, the panel will be closed; Show=Shows theScan Display panel; Hide=Hides the Scan Display panel; Show Exit Button=Shows the exit button of the Scan Display so the operator can close the display interactively; Hide Exit Button=Hides the exit button of the Scan Display so the operator can not close the display; Clear Graph=Clears the graph data within the Scan display."; Show Abort Button = Display Abort Button so the Operator can Abort the scan operation; Hide Abort Button = hide Abort Button on Display
Parameter.EnumStrings: Clear Graph,Create,Dispose,Hide,Hide Abort Button,Hide Exit Button,Show,Show Abort Button,Show Exit Button

Parameter.Name: File
Parameter.Description: Path to the LineScan data file. (TM_Path definition is allowed)

Parameter.Name: Method
Parameter.Description: Method that should be used to fit the data.

Parameter.Name: Polynomial order
Parameter.Description: Polynominal order of the polynominal function that should be used for the fit.

Parameter.Name: ResultSelect
Parameter.Description: Select which result should be returned (Minimal or Maximal value).

Parameter.Name: ShowScan?
Parameter.Description: Select whether to show a LineScan panel with the fitted data.

Parameter.Name: Result_X
Parameter.Description: This is the resulting X Position. Depending on ResultSelect, this is either the minimum or the maximum.

Parameter.Name: Result_Y
Parameter.Description: This is the resulting Y Position. Depending on ResultSelect, this is either the minimum or the maximum.

Parameter.Name: Text
Parameter.Description: Set description for input value

Statement.Description: Returns the information, whether the Cancel button was pressed by user.
Statement.Name: ProgressBar_IsCanceled

Parameter.Name: IsCanceled
Parameter.Description: Indicates, if user has pressed the Cancel button.

Statement.Description: Changes the text message within the progress bar window. Title remains unchanged.
Statement.Name: ProgressBar_Message

Parameter.Name: Message
Parameter.Description: Text message to be displayed in the progress bar window

Statement.Description: Starts the execution of the progress bar. The modal progress bar window is showed during the execution. The appearance of the progress bar is different depending of the selected mode. In Progress mode the bar fills up until the operation is done. In Wait mode a piece of bar is running through the housing again and again.
Statement.Name: ProgressBar_Start

Parameter.Name: Mode
Parameter.Description: Mode of execution: Progress mode should be used, when the duration (in terms of how many iterations will be performed until the operation is done) is known. For example: all files in a folder should be copied. In this case the amount of files is known (or can be identifed). In this mode the user can observe how many per cent of the files are already copied. The amout of files to be copied must be passed via the Scale Maximum paremeter to scale the bar. The Wait mode should be used, when the duration is unknown. In this case the Scale Maximum parameter is ignored.

Parameter.Name: Scale Maximum (Progress Mode Only)
Parameter.Description: Defines the max. amount of iterations until the operation is done. This parameter only applies in Progress mode.

Parameter.Name: Abortable
Parameter.Description: Defines if the progress bar window should disaply the Cancel button to allow the user to cancel the operation.

Parameter.Name: Message
Parameter.Description: Initial text message showed in the progress bar window. It can be changed during operation using the STMT_ProgressBar_Message function.

Parameter.Name: Title
Parameter.Description: VI Title of the progress bar window.

Statement.Description: Increments the progress bar by the value passed via Scale Increment Parameter. This function has effect only in the Progress mode and should be called every iteration step.
Statement.Name: ProgressBar_Step

Parameter.Name: Scale Increment (Progress Mode Only)
Parameter.Description: Defines the value the progress bar should be incremented. This value should be generally equal to 1.

Statement.Description: Terminates the execution of the progress bar. The modal window disapears.
Statement.Name: ProgressBar_Stop

Statement.Description: Measures a value (e.g. current) with the chosen source meter; commonly current, voltage, power or resistance.

Parameter.Name: SourceMeter

Parameter.Name: measurand

Parameter.Name: Value

Parameter.EnumStrings: Meas Range I,Meas Range U,Mode Sense,Mode Source,Source I,Source Limit I,Source Limit U,Source Range I,Source Range U,Source U

Parameter.EnumStrings: Meas Range Auto I,Meas Range Auto U,Output,Source Range Auto I,Source Range Auto U

Parameter.Name: Modus

Statement.Description: This statement activates a protective room previously configured in the Protective Room Sets panel. The room sets available can be chosen in the parameter field Room Set.
Statement.Name: ActivateRoom

Parameter.Name: Room Set
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select a Protective Room parameter set from drop down list.

Statement.Description: Applies the current positions to an existing position set. The speed and sequence of the axis remains unchanged as defined via the Jog/Shuttle dialog.
Statement.Name: ChangePositionSet

Parameter.Name: PositionSet
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select a Position parameter set from drop down list.

Statement.Description: The axis selected in the Axes name parameter field will be calibrated to its mechanical center position. The axis must be fiducialized once before rooms can be set.
Statement.Name: Fiducial

Parameter.Name: Stage name
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select stage from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Sync
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select if the fiducial process is executed synchronous to other sequences steps or not: Sync= no other sequence steps are executed until fiducial has finished; No sync= fiducial process is executed while other sequence steps are running.

Statement.Description: The axes run by the controller selected in the Controller parameter field will be calibrated to their mechanical center position. The axes must be fiducialized once before rooms can be set. Note: This statement is not available for all stages.
Statement.Name: FiducialMulti

Parameter.Name: Controller
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select a stage controller from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Sync
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select if the fiducial process is executed synchronous to other sequences steps or not: Sync= no other sequence steps are executed until fiducial process has finished; No sync= fiducial process is executed while other sequence steps are running.

Statement.Description: This statement starts the movement to a position previously defined in the Position Set panel. The position sets available can be chosen from the parameter field Position.
Statement.Name: GoToPosition

Parameter.Name: Position
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select a Position parameter set from drop down list.

Statement.Description: This statement starts movement of a stage to a relative or absolute position. The MoveAxes statement and its parameters is similar to the operation of axes from within the Jog Shuttle panel.
Statement.Name: MoveStage

Parameter.Name: Stage
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select a stage from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Velocity [um/s]
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter velocity of movement in µm/s.

Parameter.Name: Distance [um]
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter move distance in µm or absolute position.

Parameter.Name: Sync
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select if the move process is executed synchronous to other sequences steps or not: Sync= no other sequence steps are executed until move has finished; No sync= move process is executed while other sequence steps are running.

Parameter.Name: rel/abs
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select if the stage should run to an absolute position or to a position relative to the current position. Relative=stage moves to a position relative to the current position; Absolute=stage moves to an absolute position.

Statement.Description: The Query Stage statement returns the relative or absolute position of the selected axis. The message parameter returns the status of the axis. The possible states are described in the Jog Shuttle panel chapter.
Statement.Name: QueryStage

Parameter.Name: Stage
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select a stage from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: Query
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select Absolute or User offset: Absolute: stage position is retrieved as an absolute position; User Offset: stage position is retrieved as a position relative to the current position.

Parameter.Name: Position [um]
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns move distance in µm.

Parameter.Name: Message
Parameter.Description: Result: Status of axes is returned. For more information about stats please refer to the description of the JogShuttle panel.

Statement.Description: Activates/deactivates the active position control of an axis.
Statement.Name: SetStageControl

Parameter.Name: Stage name
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select a stage from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: State
Parameter.Description: Select On or Off: On: activate position control; Off: deactivate position control.

Statement.Description: This statement defines and activates an absolute motion limit for the selected axis. Setting Full range to TRUE will enable the whole motion range defined for the certain axis in the Hardware.ini file. The limits are defined in user coordinates.
Statement.Name: SetStageLimit

Parameter.Name: Stage
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select a stage from drop down list.

Parameter.Name: neg. Limit (µm)
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Define negative motion limit in µm.

Parameter.Name: pos. Limit (µm)
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Define positive motion limit in µm.

Parameter.Name: full range
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select true or false: True: enables the whole motion range defined for the axes in Hardware.ini file; deactivate SetStageLimit. False: activates SetStageLimit.

Statement.Description: This statement checks, if a stage is fiducialized or not.
Statement.Name: StageCheckAllFiducialed

Parameter.Name: Fiducialed?
Parameter.Description: Result: Returns, if stage is fiducialized (true) or not (false).

Statement.Description: This statement checks, if a selected stage is fiducialized or not; true or false is returned.
Statement.Name: StageCheckFiducialed

Parameter.Name: Stage name
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select a stage from the drop down list.

Statement.Description: Displays a simple dialogue, with Dialog text. Warning: This function should only be used in single-sequencer setups, otherwise sequences running in parallel will be interrupted until DisplayDialog has finished. Note: If possible please always use the programs dialogue function described below, because less system resources are required.
Statement.Name: DisplayDialog

Parameter.Name: Dialog text
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Dialog text to be displayed.

Parameter.Name: SelectedString

Statement.Description: Displays a simple dialog, with Title, Dialog text and Button text. The dialog can be positioned using TopPos and LeftPos in screen coordinates for the upper left corner of the dialog box. Note: If possible please always use this dialog function, because less system resources are required.
Statement.Name: DisplayExtdDialog

Parameter.Name: Dialog text

Parameter.Name: LeftPos

Parameter.Name: Title
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter the dialog title.

Parameter.Name: Button text
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter the button text.

Statement.Description: Displays a dialogue with 2 buttons, one for OK (the next sequence statement will be executed), and one for SKIP (the next statement will be skipped). The text on the buttons may be set by sequence parameters. Additionally, the position of this dialogue on the screen may be specified as TopPos and LeftPos in screen coordinates for the upper left corner of the dialogue box. Note: If possible please always use this dialogue function, because less system resources are required.
Statement.Name: DisplayExtdSelectionDialog

Parameter.Name: Dialog text
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter the dialog text to be displayed.

Parameter.Name: LeftPos
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Choose screen coordinates for the left corner of the dialog box.

Parameter.Name: TopPos
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Choose screen coordinates for the upper corner of the dialog box.

Parameter.Name: Window Title

Parameter.Name: Button 1 (OK) text
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter the text for the first button (OK).

Parameter.Name: Button 2 (Skip) text
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter the text for the second button (SKIP).

Statement.Description: This Statement opens a file dialog with the parameters defined and returns the selected path.

Parameter.Name: Button label
Parameter.Description: Label to display on the OK or Current Directory button in the file dialog box.

Parameter.Name: Default name
Parameter.Description: Name you want to appear as the initial file or directory name in the dialog box.

Parameter.Name: Pattern (all files)
Parameter.Description: Restricts the files displayed in the dialog box to those whose name matches pattern (all files). pattern (all files) does not restrict the directories displayed.

Parameter.Name: Pattern label
Parameter.Description: Label to display in the file dialog box next to the custom pattern.

Parameter.Name: Prompt
Parameter.Description: Custom message that appears as the title of the file dialog box. (Windows and Linux) The default dialog box title is Choose or Enter Path of File. (Mac OS X) The default dialog box title is Select File or Create New File.

Parameter.Name: Start path
Parameter.Description: Path of the directory whose contents LabVIEW initially displays in the dialog box.

Parameter.Name: Selected path
Parameter.Description: Full path to the file or directory selected using this dialog box.

Parameter.Name: Cancelled
Parameter.Description: Is TRUE if you cancel the dialog box.

Parameter.Name: Exists
Parameter.Description: Is TRUE if selected path specifies an existing file or directory.

Statement.Description: Displays a dialog with 2 buttons, one for OK (the next sequence statement will be executed), and one for SKIP (the next statement will be skipped). Statement.Description: Stops the execution of the current sequence. Whenever this statement is reached, the execution of the current sequence is stopped. Note: The use of EndSeq is NOT necessary at the last line of a sequence. Execution is stopped automatically. If subsequences are executed, only the subsequence is stopped.
Statement.Name: EndSeq

Statement.Description: Executes a command in a Dos window. In some cases you have to set the enviroment path to the folder of the excecutable or you have to set the full pass to the excecutable in the command.
Statement.Name: ExecDos

Parameter.Name: Command Line
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select command line.

Parameter.Name: working directory
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Select working directory.

Parameter.Name: wait until completion
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Enter true or false. If wait until completion? is TRUE, STANDARD INPUT is available as input to the command and STANDARD OUTPUT and STANDARD ERROR are available when the command finishes running. If FALSE, the command runs in the background and its input and output are not available.

Statement.Description: Executes a command in a Dos window. In some cases you have to set the enviroment path to the folder of the excecutable or you have to set the full pass to the excecutable in the command.

If wait until completion? is TRUE, STANDARD INPUT is available as input to the command and STANDARD OUTPUT and STANDARD ERROR are available when the command finishes running. If FALSE, the command runs in the background and its input and output are not available.

Parameter.Name: Parameter

Statement.Description: Starts the execution of the sequence. Provide the full path to the sequence to be called. The next statement will be executed without waiting for the started sequence to finish.
Statement.Name: ExecSeqAsync

Parameter.Name: Sequence
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Choose a sequence to be executed from the opening file dialog box.

Statement.Description: This statement executes all sequences contained in the folder specified. The complete path to the folder has to be provided. The sequences within the folder will be executed in uprising order of their names. The sequence are executed synchronized which means that the next sequence is started when the sequence before has finished execution.
Statement.Name: ExecSeqFolder

Parameter.Name: Folder
Parameter.Description: Parameter: Choose a folder from the opening file dialog box.

Statement.Description: Computes the exponetial value x^y.
Statement.Name: Exponent
